patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.21.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.21.0
**Date:** 2026-07-06
**Previous release:** v0.20.0

v0.21.0 is a systems-and-polish release with **72 commits** since v0.20.0. The headline is the first full gathering/professions pass: gatherable world nodes, per-player node respawns, corpse harvesting, proficiency-scaled material rarity, crafted tool tiers, and a HUD that shows node state and gathering proficiency. This release also makes Thunzharr far harder to kite, adds painted equipment icons, gives admins role-based dashboard permissions and live bot-detector tuning, improves nameplate and corpse picking, adds Meta CAPI tracking, and brings the full release localization gate back to zero pending keys.

## Highlights

- **Gathering and professions:** gatherable herb/ore/logging nodes now exist in the world with render props, per-player harvest state, respawn timers, proficiency tracking, rarity rolls, monster-material corpse harvests, and crafted tool tiers (#1175, #1181, #1183, #1190, #1191, #1194).
- **Gathering HUD:** node interaction now has localized HUD feedback for node state, gathering skill, and proficiency progress (#1194).
- **Thunzharr tightened up:** the world boss now spawns at realm boot, respawns on a 90-minute cadence, scales visually and mechanically, uses a Howling Gale snare to punish kiting, and has a cleaner raid lockout source of truth (#1427, #1446).
- **Painted equipment icons:** armor and equipment now join consumables/resources with committed painted item art, with mapping coverage and tests (#1406).
- **Admin dashboard hardening:** fine-grained role-based permissions, staff management, in-game moderation permissions, and runtime bot-detector configuration/history controls land together (#1433, #1455).
- **Server-side analytics:** Meta CAPI events add server-side tracking alongside the client pixel path (#1460).
- **Rendering polish:** stacked nameplates declutter instead of overlapping, stacked corpse picks disambiguate, Lightning Bolt gets a bolt-shaped electric projectile and custom cast loop, and class defaults get clearer priest/mage silhouettes (#1259, #1388, #1445, #1466).
- **Release localization:** all new gathering/admin strings are translated, generated i18n artifacts are fresh, and the release-tier empty-pending gate is green (#1472).

## Gathering and Professions

- **Gatherable world nodes:** gather nodes are placed in the world and rendered with dedicated props, lookup tables, screenshots, tests, and zone-band verification (#1175).
- **Per-player node harvest and respawn:** each character gets their own node harvest state and respawn timer, so one player collecting a node does not consume another player's opportunity (#1183).
- **Proficiency tracking:** gathering skill progression is tracked per player, surfaced through the world API, and displayed in the HUD (#1194).
- **Material rarity rolls:** gathering outcomes now scale with proficiency, with deterministic tests pinning RNG draw accounting and rarity constants (#1190).
- **Corpse harvesting:** monster component tags and single-use first-come corpse harvesting add a second source of profession materials, including claim reset on respawn (#1181).
- **Crafted tool tiers:** harvesting respects crafted tool tiers and monster-material tier gates, with profession tool tests covering the rules (#1191).

## World Boss

- **Boot spawn:** Thunzharr now rises at realm boot instead of waiting for a later schedule tick (#1427).
- **Harder to kite:** Howling Gale applies a snare, the encounter self-anchors more aggressively, and the boss was reworked through several HP/scale passes before the final 90-minute respawn tuning (#1446).
- **Raid lockout cleanup:** world-boss loot lockouts now use one source of truth, making the daily lockout easier to reason about and test.
- **Visual clarity:** Thunzharr size and presence were increased, with before/after screenshots committed for review.

## Admin, Moderation, and Analytics

- **Role-based dashboard access:** admin routes, page visibility, and in-game moderation actions now route through fine-grained permissions instead of broad all-or-nothing admin access (#1455).
- **Staff management:** the admin dashboard can manage staff roles, display role-change history, and protect superadmin grants through the grant script (#1455).
- **Runtime bot-detector configuration:** operators can adjust bot-detector thresholds live, review history, reload prior versions into the editor, and persist overrides across server restarts (#1433).
- **Meta CAPI:** server-side Meta conversion events backstop client analytics and are covered by API tests (#1460).
- **Desktop launcher preparation:** the site and release plumbing now include the desktop launcher download path (#1461).

## UI, Rendering, and Content

- **Painted equipment icons:** equipment and armor item ids map to committed `public/ui/items/*.webp` assets, with `src/ui/icons.ts` preferring painted art where present and falling back to procedural recipes otherwise (#1406).
- **Nameplate decluttering:** overlapping nameplates stack instead of painting directly over each other (#1466).
- **Corpse pick disambiguation:** stacked corpse interactions are easier to select correctly (#1259).
- **Gathering minimap/HUD polish:** gathering node state and proficiency appear in the HUD, and minimap/mail badge placement was adjusted so indicators no longer pile onto the same rim slot (#1194, #1436).
- **Lightning Bolt visuals:** shaman Lightning Bolt now has a custom cast loop and a bolt-shaped electric projectile (#1445).
- **Aura readability:** player debuffs moved into a clearer row and get WoW-style school-tinted borders (#1431).
- **Character select fixes:** the account bar no longer covers the roster, and the v0.20.0 character-screen chrome fixes remain in place (#1432).
- **Content tuning:** starter characters receive rations, early-zone boar/spider/wolf HP was retuned, T1 epic legs/boots are grindable in Thornpeak Heights, and the Gravewyrm Sanctum entrance moved off the mountain (#1426, #1428, #1429).

## Localization

- The v0.21.0 release fill translated the new gathering HUD and admin/staff/bot-detector strings across the supported locales, taking the registry to `pending=0`.
- Dialect overlays continue to inherit from their base locales; duplicate `es_ES`/`fr_CA` gathering overlay entries were removed so the dialect-resolution tests stay meaningful.
- The generated game/admin resolved tables, pending modules, status summary, and `src/ui/i18n.resolved.sha256` baseline were regenerated for this release.

## Validation

Release PR #1472 (`release/v0.21.0` into `main`) merged as commit `914b5c659`, which is this release's tag target. The following GitHub checks passed on the release branch before merge:

- Release gate (full 14-locale, `I18N_RELEASE_TIER=1`)
- PR gate (English-only legal)
- Release version gate
- Format + lint (Biome, changed files)
- Screenshots of changes
- AI review

Local validation before the final release push also passed:

- `I18N_RELEASE_TIER=1 npm test`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npm run security:gate`
- `npm run build:env`
- `npm run build:server`
- `npm run build`

## Upgrade Notes

- **Database:** this release adds admin/staff and bot-detector configuration persistence, plus profession/gathering-related state through existing character/world save surfaces. The server schema changes are additive and idempotent.
- **Environment:** Meta CAPI and bot-detector/runtime configuration integrations are safe when unset; optional environment variables should be configured only for deployments using those integrations.
- **Localization:** pull the committed generated i18n artifacts as-is; do not regenerate them manually during deploy.
- **Native versions:** package, Android, and iOS version metadata are bumped to `0.21.0`.

## Size

464 files changed, 21,166 insertions, 2,419 deletions versus v0.20.0. Major changed areas: professions/gathering simulation and UI, world-boss tuning, admin dashboard permissions and bot-detector configuration, equipment icon assets, localization artifacts, rendering/nameplate fixes, analytics, and release/version infrastructure.

## Major PRs and Workstreams Included

- #1472 Release v0.21.0
- #1466 Nameplate decluttering
- #1461 Desktop launcher download preparation
- #1460 Meta CAPI server-side analytics
- #1455 Admin role-based permissions and staff management
- #1446 Thunzharr anti-kite and respawn tuning
- #1445 Lightning Bolt projectile and cast loop
- #1433 Runtime bot-detector configuration
- #1428 Grindable T1 epic legs/boots
- #1427 World boss boot spawn
- #1406 Painted equipment icons
- #1259 Stacked corpse pick disambiguation
- #1194 Gathering HUD and proficiency display
- #1191 Crafted tool tiers and material tier gating
- #1190 Proficiency-scaled material rarity
- #1183 Per-player node harvest and respawn
- #1181 Corpse harvesting
- #1175 Gatherable node placements and render props

## Contributors

Thanks to the contributors represented in the release range: Reuben Horne, Ryan Foo, Matthieu, Trev Cavill, Donny-Deals, jgyy, Blaine, Humpalumps, Enrique García-Ferrer, and others whose work landed through the release branch.

patches.name: v0.20.0
patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.20.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.20.0
**Date:** 2026-07-04
**Previous release:** v0.19.0

v0.20.0 is the biggest content release yet, with **379 commits** since v0.19.0. The headline is The Drowned Litany, a second delve set in a flooded Mirefen marsh ruin, arriving alongside the Ravenpost mail system with a calendar and guild events, Thunzharr the first world boss, bags, haste as a real stat, a browser map editor, and a complete character-select redesign. Under the hood this cycle also completes the IP pivot rename sweep, fills every one of the 21 locales to zero pending keys, and tightens account security from recovery-email capture to a hardened AI-review pipeline.

## Highlights

- **The Drowned Litany:** a second delve (levels 12-14) beneath the Mirefen marsh: seven flooded modules with Blackwater hazards, a bell-rite puzzle, the spider-infested Sinkhole Baptistry, Sister Nhalia as the boss, and Edda Reedhand as the delve's healer companion (#1327).
- **Ravenpost mail, calendar, and guild events:** send coin and items to any character (online or off) with real escrow, delivery delay, and postage; plus a calendar window and guild events backed by new server tables (#1339).
- **Thunzharr, the first world boss:** a daily personal-loot epic encounter dropping Tier-2 set gloves and belts (#1034).
- **Haste:** melee, ranged, and spell haste via the item-set bonus framework: every Tier-2 3-piece now grants 15% haste, and three new leveling haste kits assemble from world drops (#994).
- **Bags:** equippable bag slots expand inventory capacity, with bag drops seeded into existing loot tables and a filterable, searchable bag grid (#1354).
- **Map editor:** build your own custom maps in the browser at /editor, with server-side per-account map storage, asset uploads, and 2D/3D navigation; playtest runs as an offline world and the live world is untouched (#1306).
- **Character select redesigned:** a full-screen WoW-style roster with the 3D character on the landing backdrop, class details docked left, the character list docked right, and (on desktop) the site menu bar kept above the stage with the wallet and developer sections as tidy cards (#1387, #1416).
- **One online character per account:** with Ravenpost mail covering cross-character transfers, the old two-session dual-boxing allowance is gone; GMs stay exempt (#1414).
- **Every locale filled:** all 640 pending keys translated across the 17 base locales (~7,850 rows), pending=0 in both the game and admin registries, and the full 14-locale release gate green.

## The Drowned Litany

- **The second delve (#1327):** The Drowned Litany is a marsh-ruin delve for levels 12-14 under Mirefen: seven modules (sluice, ledger, ring, baptistry, choir loft, causeway, and the boss apse) with non-rectangular flooded layouts, static Blackwater hazard zones that damage anyone standing in them, and a Gravecaller flood-cult theme.
- **Enemy kit:** Reedbound Acolytes hurl Rotwater Vials from the back of the pack, Drowned Cantors shield their allies, Choir Thralls and Tolling Bells drive the bell mechanics, Mirefen Widowlings snare with Web Snare, Deepfen Spearjaws frenzy when fed, Sump Troll Devourers self-shield and stomp, and Grave-Silt Bulwarks cleave as CC-immune elites.
- **The bell rite:** a shrine-sequence puzzle gates the reliquary: shrines replay their rite sequence, correct rings advance it, wrong rings splash Blackwater at your feet, and the bell ropes shock Drowned Cantors out of their canticle.
- **Sinkhole Baptistry:** spider egg-sacs cling to the flooded rim and burst into spiderlings; the finale wave stirs from the black water.
- **Sister Nhalia, the Drowned Canticle:** the boss marks players with Blackwater, tolls the bells, and shields behind her cantors; her encounter state fully resets on evade (#1408).
- **Edda Reedhand:** the delve's healer companion joins the run, ranks up with Delve Marks (rank 3 grants a once-per-run ally revive), and now uses the druid player rig with a staff and her authored backpack, giving her the full animation set including a real Spellcasting channel for her heals (#1415).
- **New models with real animation integration:** the Reedbound Acolyte swapped from a placeholder mesh to the Stone Cantor model from the Raid 02 asset batch (#1415); a synthesized hit-react flinch was authored into the rig so it reacts to blows (#1418); and the vial throw is now telegraphed: the sim emits a windup, the cast animation leads, and the projectile leaves the hand exactly at the clip's release pose, with cadence unchanged (#1421).
- Ranged mobs that hurl instant bolts (the acolyte, warlock demons) now animate the launch; previously a caster stood statue-still while its projectile flew (#1418).
- A second delve means delve affixes, companions, the delve shop, and mid-delve pet stow all apply across both delves; delve wall/collider verification scripts cover the new modules.

## Ravenpost Mail, Calendar, and Guild Events

- **Player mail (#1339):** send a letter with coin and up to three item stacks to any character; goods leave escrow immediately, ride the raven for the delivery delay, and postage is charged on send. Recipient boxes cap per recipient; quest items and unlistable items cannot be mailed.
- **Escrow hardening (#1411):** the take/delete/blocked-sender paths and the unread index were hardened against races and abuse.
- **Mailbox UX (#1413):** the coin fields select their contents on focus (typing 1 means 1g, not 10g appended to the seeded 0), and sending or collecting mail repaints the bags window immediately instead of on close.
- **Calendar and guild events (#1339):** a calendar window with guild event creation and removal, backed by a new guild_events table.
- **Demons are not free:** a warlock's summoned demon is deliberately not persisted across logout, so a relog cannot launder the summon cooldown.

## World Boss, Haste, and Loot

- **Thunzharr (#1034):** the first world boss, with daily personal loot (Tier-2 epic set gloves and belts) tracked per character per day.
- **Haste (#994):** one haste stat drives melee, ranged, and spell speed: spell haste divides cast time and channel duration (tick count unchanged, GCD deliberately not hasted), melee/ranged haste divide swing intervals. Every Tier-2 3-piece bonus (Crownforged, Nighttalon, Soulflame, Stormcaller's) now also grants 15% haste, and three leveling kits (Vale Arcanist's Regalia, Boundstone Vanguard, Greyjaw Stalker's Kit) grant haste alone at 3 pieces. Ability tooltips and the spellbook show the real hasted cast time, online too.
- **Bags (#1354):** equippable bags expand carry capacity, with a filter bar, search, and per-bag socket display; wolfhide satchels drop from zone wolves and dungeon bonus rolls seed the higher tiers.
- **Walk-by autoloot (#1261):** an opt-in proximity autoloot with round-robin party loot; the default party loot strategy for common items flips from looter-takes-all to round-robin.
- **Loot rights and locks:** the shared rights shape from autoloot composes with the world boss's personal daily lockout.
- **Item-instance plumbing (#1174):** inventory slots can now carry a per-instance payload (groundwork for future crafting); the World Market and Ravenpost mail count and consume only fungible stock, so an instanced copy can never be laundered into a plain stack.

## Combat, Death, and the Sim

- **Character-bound combo points (#1367):** combo points now live on the character, not the target, so target-swapping no longer erases them; the duplicate combo row on the target frame is gone (#1389).
- **Aggro before damage (#1324):** auto-attacking no longer generates aggro before damage lands, with an option and manual toggle; merged with the ranged-aggro timing pass (#1343) so both hold: melee-only engage aggro seed plus the no-aggro-while-casting gate.
- **Ranged aggro and physical projectiles (#1343):** ranged mob aggro timing fixed and physical shots became real projectiles.
- **Ghost mode (#1384):** dying leaves a corpse and a ghost: run your spirit back to your corpse, or accept the Spirit Healer's resurrection with Resurrection Sickness; new graveyards dot all three zones. Dead players cannot interact with the world, and logging out preserves the death loop (#1407).
- **Cast and channel target locking (#1395):** casts and channels lock their target at cast start.
- **Evade immunity (#1403):** pets no longer inherit evade immunity from their targets.
- **Line of sight over low props (#1379):** ranged attacks and spells path over low scenery instead of being blocked by fences and crates.
- **Unclimbable mountains (#1325):** mob locomotion respects steep terrain.
- **Arena wall tunneling (#1109):** players can no longer knockback-tunnel through arena walls.

## Map Editor

- **A browser custom-map editor (#1306):** /editor ships as its own bundle entry: sculpt terrain and place props, camps, and NPCs with 2D pan/zoom and 3D orbit/fly navigation, region tools, and an asset drawer, with server-side map storage (new maps and user_assets tables, auth-gated, capped per account) and JSON export/import.
- **Playtest is offline-only by design:** a playtest hands the custom world to the local offline Sim via a same-origin session handoff; it never touches the server or the authoritative world, and the editor's code never enters the shipped game bundle.
- The editor is fully localized (its 338 strings are the largest block of this release's translation fill).

## Character Select and HUD

- **WoW-style character select (#1387):** the roster becomes a full-screen stage: your character in 3D over the landing backdrop, class details docked left, the character list docked right, world and sort selectors in the account bar.
- **Desktop chrome (#1416):** the site menu bar (Play / High Scores / Wiki / News / Account) stays visible above the character screens, and the $WOC Wallet and Developer sections became two matching stacked cards with their captions at the foot.
- **Unit frame auras (#1374) and customization (#1369):** party and target frames show auras, and unit frames gain customization options.
- **Chat window tabs (#1394):** the chat window's tabs were fixed and rounded out; whispers keep their dedicated filter tab.
- **Quest direction indicators (#1316):** the tracker points you at objectives (implements #1315), with keyboard-focus and keybind-leak fixes; quest progress flashes on update (#1376); ground-spawn quest objects are hidden from players not on the quest (#1404 carried to v0.21).
- **Responsive windows (#1317):** HUD windows adapt to viewport size.
- **Gamepad:** brand-correct button glyphs (#1373), and no pad input is taken while the window is unfocused (#1318).
- **Spawn intro cinematic (#1330):** a short camera flight introduces the world on spawn.
- **Own nameplate (#1287):** optionally show your own overhead nameplate (off by default, per #1235).
- **Daily rewards:** a daily leaderboard page (#1338), task feedback (#1350), repeatable quest tasks (#1392), a WOC disclaimer (#1397), and a chest shortcut toggle with a confirm dialog before hiding (#1328, #1420).
- **Mob hover tooltips** and high-contrast text fixes on the Parchment theme (#1107); mouse-camera facing commits its final yaw on release (#1151).

## Accounts, Platform, and Security

- **Recovery email capture (#1363):** registration requires a valid email; pre-email accounts get a blocking backfill prompt at sign-in; the Discord OAuth flow captures the verified address.
- **One online character per account (#1414):** the anti-multibox cap drops from two live sessions to one now that mail moves goods between characters; GMs stay exempt for supervision.
- **Global inbound WebSocket rate limit (#1108):** a hard server-side message-rate cap per connection.
- **Play Integrity nonce normalization (#1422):** Android attestation nonce handling normalized.
- **Global party invite (#981):** invite by name from anywhere via a slash command; blocked players cannot party-invite or guild-invite you (#1410).
- **Guild roster last-login (#1378)** and an admin evidences search page for the bot detector (#1383), plus a live evidence view.
- **AI review pipeline hardened (#1409, #1390, #1393):** the PR review agent runs with a minimal allowlisted environment (never GITHUB_TOKEN or the OAuth secret), its public output is scrubbed through a secret redactor, and the reworked verifying reviewer checks out the PR head, runs tsc and the covering vitest files before writing, with inline visual-only screenshots.
- **CI secrets:** the workflow no longer leaks secrets into the review agent (#1409); the Docker image build was fixed for the fifth bundle entry (#1412).

## IP Pivot

- **The rename sweep lands (#1341):** every remaining legacy name moved to original IP across abilities, talents, items, mobs, warlock demons (Emberkin, Gloomshade, Duskborn, Spellhound, Warfiend, Pyre Colossus, Wraithborn), world text, and all 21 locale overlays. Every rename is display-only: ids are frozen, so saves, the wire protocol, and the RL env are untouched, and the parity goldens prove it.

## Localization

- **Zero pending keys:** the release fill translated all 640 pending keys (614 game + 26 admin) across the 17 base locales, roughly 7,850 rows: the map editor, mail and bags HUD strings, the Drowned Litany's entities and delve UI, and the admin calibration and suspicious-players pages. Dialect locales (es_ES, fr_CA, en_CA) resolve through their bases.
- Three IP-pivot ability names (Frostveil, Duskfire, Oakhide) that had been copied verbatim into the French, German, and Indonesian overlays are now properly translated, and the Drowned Litany's bell-toll boss line is localized in every sim locale table.
- The full I18N_RELEASE_TIER=1 gate (14-locale key parity, empty pending set, copied-English checks, S3 emit recognition) is green.

## Fixes and Infrastructure

- The Dockerfile now copies editor.html, so the v0.20.0 container image builds again; verified end to end against a blank Postgres (#1412).
- Housekeeping (#1340, #1371) was reverted out of the release (#1398) for a later cut.
- The loot-master 5-minute-timeout test flake was fixed (#1349) and the i18n registry expect-storm tamed (#1377).
- Wallet extension help (#1344) and the AI-review env model override (#1347).
- Discord: the Admin role shows in game and the Artist link is restored (#1303).

patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.19.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.19.0  
**Date:** 2026-07-03  
**Previous release:** v0.18.0  

v0.19.0 is a large gameplay and platform release with **59 commits** since v0.18.0. The headline is Talents 2.0, a casting overhaul that lands the deterministic primitives the choice-row talent redesign is built on (interrupt with a PvP consent gate, empower-next charges, and cast-while-moving) alongside a new ground-targeted casting mode with five aimed AoE spells. This cycle also ships wallet-gated Daily Rewards with a real-USD daily leaderboard, an Electron desktop app plus native store-update prompts, a client hot-path performance pass, whisper quality-of-life, and a from-scratch soundtrack recomposition.

## Highlights

- **Talents 2.0 casting foundation:** v0.19.0 lands the deterministic primitives for the choice-row talent redesign, interrupt (with a PvP consent gate and lockout diminishing returns), empower-next charges (an instant, a free, and a guaranteed-crit next cast), and cast-while-moving, all content-unused so the parity goldens stay byte-identical (#1305).
- **Ground-targeted casting:** five caster and ranged classes gain aimed AoE (mage Flamestrike, warlock Rain of Fire, hunter Volley, druid Hurricane, shaman Earthquake) on a new server-clamped targetMode 'position' primitive, with a desktop cursor reticle and a terrain-draped AoE impact ring (#1064).
- **Daily Rewards:** Players holding enough WOC in a verified wallet now earn points from a daily weighted spin (20 to 250) and server-credited quest, arena, and online-time tasks, then climb a per-realm daily leaderboard whose top 10 split a real-USD prize pool (20/15/12/10/9/8/7.5/7/6.5/5 percent), with the winners posted to Discord once per day (#1307).
- **Native store updates:** iOS and Android now prompt players to update through the App Store or Play Store, and the old Appflow over-the-air bundle swap is turned off (#1288, #1286).
- **Performance:** A client hot-path pass froze 1252 static terrain and prop matrices, fixed the scene root that defeated the freeze, warmed icons off the main thread, and trimmed 20 Hz decode and per-frame allocations, lifting an in-crowd measurement from 79.9 to 82.5 fps (#1254).
- **Soundtrack recomposed:** Every non-Eastbrook music cue was rewritten around per-place leitmotifs and multi-section forms, from Mirefen's waterlogged requiem to a rebuilt "Vanguard" battle theme, while the three Eastbrook Vale themes stay note-for-note identical and are now checksum-pinned (#1291).
- **Whispers rounded out:** a filter-only Whisper tab collects every whisper in one place and replies to the last whisperer with /r, a Whisper action lands in the portrait right-click menu, and a dev-only /dev bot spawns a whisperable auto-replying test dummy to exercise it all offline (#1313).

## Talents 2.0: Casting Overhaul and Ground Targeting

- **The casting foundation for the choice-row talent redesign.** v0.19.0 lands the deterministic casting primitives the Talents 2.0 rework is built on (design locked in docs/prd/talents-2.0.md: six class-wide choice rows at levels 5, 8, 11, 14, 17, and 20 that replace the old 234-node point trees). The primitives ship content-unused this cycle, so no row grants them yet and the 159 parity goldens are unmodified, but every future interrupt, mobility, and empowerment talent now has a real mechanic underneath it instead of a flat stat nudge (#1305).
- **Interrupt (#1305):** a new interrupt ability effect cancels a target's non-physical, non-fishing cast and applies the existing school lockout aura for the effect's lockout duration. The victim's cast resolves per-player when possible (rank and mods) with a global ABILITIES fallback, so a future mob caster is interruptible too, while a scripted pseudo-cast that is absent from the ability table resolves to nothing and stays immune by design.
- The interrupt primitive was hardened before any interrupt content ships: interrupting a non-consenting player outside a duel, arena, or fiesta (through the shared isHostileTo hostility model) is a full no-op with no cancel and no lockout; lockout durations route through diminishing returns under a new lockout DR category (full, half, quarter, immune on an 18 second reset window), and on DR immunity the cast still cancels but no lockout lands; and a new AbilityDef.uninterruptible flag protects an explicitly flagged cast (#1305).
- **Empower-next (#1305):** three one-charge self-auras in src/sim/combat/empower_next.ts. next_cast_instant makes the next cast-time spell instant (never a channel, never a physical cast); next_cast_free waives its cost, consumed exactly where the cost is billed (queue time for on-next-swing, cast start for channels and instants, completion for cast-time spells, and the conjure and revive early branches); and next_attack_crit forces the next damaging roll to crit while consuming the identical rng draw, so draw order and count are unchanged.
- **Cast-while-moving (#1305):** a new AbilityDef.castWhileMoving flag plus a per-ability talent transform (the Firestarter design) baked onto the resolved KnownAbility, so a flagged cast or channel survives the player's own movement. The move-input cast cancel skips a mobile cast, and fishing still cancels.
- **Conditional-effect primitives (#1305, also content-unused):** directDamage gains vsRootedMult (multiplies a hit against a rooted target, applied after the spell-power rider and before the crit multiplier), and a new critVsRooted global adds spell crit chance against rooted targets, read through the playerMods accessor so fiesta augment overlays compose; the crit roll stays a single rng draw with a different chance input. addEffects lets a talent append effect riders to an ability, shallow-copied before the scaling pass so they inherit the same modifiers as native effects and shared content arrays are never mutated.
- **Ground-targeted casting (#1064):** a new targetMode 'position' aims an ability at a world point instead of an entity. The client proposes the point and the server clamps it to the ability's range from the caster (authoritative) through a new castAt command; the cast's area effects (aoeDamage and groundAoE) center on that point, and with no point chosen the cast falls back to the caster's feet so existing callers are unaffected. The aim is stashed in Entity.castAim, clamped at cast start, and always cleared on resolve, cancel, or channel completion.
- Five thematic ground spells ship on the primitive, one per caster and ranged class, each fully translated across every locale with its own distinct procedural icon (src/ui/icons.ts): mage Flamestrike (learned at level 20), warlock Rain of Fire, hunter Volley, druid Hurricane, and shaman Earthquake (all learned at level 18) (#1064).
- Flamestrike is an instant aimed burst rather than a lingering zone (owner feedback: no dot on the floor): it detonates once at the range-clamped point for 34 to 44 Fire damage in a 7 yard radius (80 mana, 12 second cooldown, 30 yard range) and leaves no ground zone (#1064).
- Rain of Fire, Volley, and Hurricane are channeled ground AoEs that rain damage on the aimed area over the channel: Rain of Fire (fire, 4 ticks over 4 seconds, 14 to 18 per tick in a 7 yard radius), Volley (physical, scales off Ranged Attack Power, 6 ticks over 3 seconds, 12 to 16 per tick in an 8 yard radius, mitigated by armor), and Hurricane (nature, 6 ticks over 6 seconds, 12 to 16 per tick in an 8 yard radius). applyChannelTick pulses a position-targeted channel's AoE at castAim every tick, with physical channels mitigated by armor and spell-school channels not, mirroring the instant aoeDamage path (#1064).
- Earthquake stays an instant lingering nature zone (groundAoE, 13 to 17 Nature damage in an 8 yard radius over 6 seconds, pulsing every 1.5 seconds) (#1064).
- **Desktop ground-targeting reticle (#1064):** pressing a position-targeted ability opens aiming mode, a school-colored pulsing reticle ring that follows the cursor on the terrain via the click-to-move ground raycast, pre-clamped to the ability's range with the sim's own clamp math (dimmed when the raw cursor is out of range) and sized to the blast radius. Left-click or the same slot keybind commits castAbilityAt at the reticle, Escape or right-click cancels, and other actions cancel aiming first. The pure aim math lives in src/ui/ground_aim.ts with unit tests, behind a new Ground-Targeting Reticle option (Interface, on by default); mobile and reticle-off keep the quick-cast-at-target-or-feet behavior.
- The impact reads for the moment it matters and then gets out of the way: a new world-anchored spellfxAt event carries the blast radius, and the renderer flashes a school-colored, terrain-draped AoE ring under the burst (a pooled visual with a pure animation model in src/render/aoe_ring.ts, 0.7 second lifetime, mirroring click_marker.ts) with no lingering decal (#1064).
- **IWorld parity and determinism:** a new castAbilityAt(abilityId, aim) on IWorldCombat is implemented in both the offline Sim and the online ClientWorld (which sends the castAt command frame), so render and ui reach ground targeting only through IWorld and the world_api parity test is updated. The primitives draw no new rng and leave the 159 parity goldens byte-identical (#1305, #1064).
- Ground-spell impacts interest-scope correctly: the server's event anchor now anchors a world-coordinate spellfxAt event at its own x and z, so an impact reaches only players within EVENT_RADIUS instead of broadcasting realm-wide, exactly like its entity-anchored siblings (#1064).

## Daily Rewards

- **Wallet-gated daily rewards (#1307):** A new daily rewards system lets players who hold enough WOC in a verified wallet earn points from a daily spin and rotating tasks, then climb a daily leaderboard for a share of a real-USD prize pool. Eligibility is checked server-side as wallet WOC balance times the WOC to USD price against a configurable minimum (default 20 USD), and every reason (eligible, no_wallet, under_minimum, price_unavailable) is surfaced to the client so a locked wallet shows exactly why.
- **One spin per day, weighted outcomes:** The spin draws one of eight outcomes worth 20, 30, 40, 50, 75, 100, 150, or 250 points, at weights 25, 22, 18, 14, 9, 6, 4, and 2 (percent) respectively. A spin is one per account per reward day, enforced by the daily_reward_spins primary key and an ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING insert, so the endpoint returns 403 when the wallet is locked and 409 when the day's spin is already claimed.
- **Server-credited tasks:** Points from tasks are only ever credited server-side, never on a client's word. Completing a quest turn-in credits the default quest task (base 10 points), an arena match credits win or loss task points, and each credited minute of activity scales a task multiplier from 1 up to 3 (one step per 30 minutes online). All task credit is gated on wallet eligibility and made idempotent by a per-source key (for example task:<taskId>:quest:<questId>), and only players active within the configured window (default 120 seconds) accrue online minutes, recorded once per UTC minute on a 60-second server sweep.
- **Daily leaderboard and USD payout splits:** Points feed a per-realm daily leaderboard (tiebreak: earliest to reach the score). At day rollover the previous day is finalized and its top 10 are written to daily_reward_payouts with fixed prize splits of 20, 15, 12, 10, 9, 8, 7.5, 7, 6.5, and 5 percent of the pool (summing to 100 percent), against a configurable pool (default 150 USD). The game server records payout rows as pending only: it never transfers funds. An external private payout service reads pending rows via POST /internal/daily-rewards/pending-payouts and reports each result back with a transaction signature via /internal/daily-rewards/mark-payout, both guarded by a dedicated shared secret.
- **Discord winner announcements (#1307):** Once a day is finalized, the community bot posts a single gold embed titled "Top N Winners" to a dedicated achievements channel, listing each winner's rank, name, points, USD prize, and percent share alongside the realm and prize pool, then marks the day announced (daily_reward_days.discord_announced_at) so it is posted exactly once. If DISCORD_DAILY_REWARDS_CHANNEL_ID is unset the bot logs once and skips the feed. The winners feed is served over the existing secret-gated /internal/discord/* surface.
- **Runtime config lives off-server:** The reward minimum, prize pool, WOC and SOL prices, and the task list are owned by the private payout service and fetched from its GET /daily-config, cached with a configurable TTL (default 5 minutes). When the service origin is unset or unreachable the server falls back to safe built-in defaults (20 USD minimum, 150 USD pool, a single quest task) and logs the reason at most once a minute, so the feature degrades gracefully rather than failing.
- **Client-asserted task credit removed before ship:** An intermediate POST /api/daily-rewards/task route that credited a task's points on the client's assertion alone was removed as a security hardening, since points drive a real-USD leaderboard. The public API exposes only status (GET /api/daily-rewards), the spin (POST /api/daily-rewards/spin), and history (GET /api/daily-rewards/history); the daily_reward_task_completions table is retained (additive, with no writer) for task-completed display and a future server-verified completion path.
- **Configurable reward day start (#1310):** The reward day no longer flips at UTC midnight. A configurable day start (dayStartUtcMinutes, default 1260 minutes, that is 21:00 UTC) shifts the boundary, and a shared dailyRewardClock resolves the active reward day from that offset across status, spin, online, quest, and arena crediting and the finalize-previous-day rollover. New rewardDayForDate, addRewardDays, and nextUtcResetIso helpers (unit-tested) compute the day and the exact next reset instant.
- **Live reset countdown (#1310):** The rewards window now shows an "Ends in" countdown next to the reset time, repainted every 30 seconds and formatted as "<1m", "{m}m", or "{h}h {m}m" from the server's resetAt, so players can see how long is left in the current cycle.
- **Client surface and launcher (#1307):** IWorld gains dailyRewards, spinDailyReward, and dailyRewardHistory (src/world_api/daily_rewards.ts), implemented in ClientWorld over REST and stubbed as a locked, no-wallet status in the offline Sim so render and ui stay world-agnostic. A treasure-chest launcher button and the rewards window are mirrored into both the index and play entries; the button pulses "spin-ready" while a spin is unclaimed or the wallet is not yet eligible, and both are hidden entirely on the native mobile app.

## UI, Rendering, Input, and Performance

- **Home graphics selector (#1284):** The landing/start screen gains a Graphics dropdown, beside the language picker and the High Contrast toggle, so players pick a tier before entering the world instead of hunting through the Esc options. The choices are Auto, Low, Medium, High, Ultra, and Advanced, mapping to `graphicsPreset` 1 to 5 in `src/game/settings.ts` (Medium is the shipped default). Auto leaves `graphicsDefaultApplied` false so the renderer keeps probing a device-appropriate tier on each boot; picking a specific preset sets `graphicsDefaultApplied` true so first-run detection never overrides the choice. An out-of-range or unrecognized stored value normalizes back to Auto (`normalizedLandingGraphicsChoice`), and the control stays gameplay-neutral: presets only shed cosmetic richness, so choosing a lower tier before login never changes actionable information.
- **Painted resource and consumable item icons (#1202):** Resource and consumable items now render committed artwork instead of procedural glyphs. A new image-icon path (`ITEM_IMAGE_IDS` plus `itemImageUrl` in `src/ui/icons.ts`) mirrors the skill-icon system and is served for kind `item` across bags, tooltips, loot, vendor, and the /wiki guide; `iconDataUrl` prefers a weapon icon, then the item image, then the procedural recipe. 101 curated 128px webp icons under `public/ui/items/` cover food, drink, potion, elixir, junk, quest, and tool items (provenance and license in `public/ui/items/mapping.json`). Equipment stays on procedural icons, and any item id not in the set falls through to `ITEM_RECIPES`. Guarded by `tests/item_icons.test.ts`.
- **Hunter pet-bar icons no longer borrow class spell art (#1202):** The pet action buttons reused real class ability icon ids (`rejuvenation`, `defensive_stance`, `rapid_fire`, `growl`, `prowl`, `drain_life`), so a hunter's Aggressive stance rendered the same icon as their own Rapid Fire and Heal Pet showed the druid's green magic heal. A new `pet_action_icons.ts` module maps each command to a dedicated recipe (`pet_attack`, `pet_growl`, `pet_feed`, `pet_mend`, `pet_passive`, `pet_defensive`, `pet_aggressive`), and Heal Pet is renamed Feed Pet with a roasted-meat icon (hunters feed pets, they do not magic-heal them); the warlock still mends its demon. Guarded by `tests/pet_action_icons.test.ts`.
- **Gamepad presses count toward APM (#1311):** The perf overlay's actions-per-minute readout previously tracked only keyboard and mouse. The `GamepadManager` now fires an `onInputEdge` callback on every button rising edge (both in-world action dispatch and the virtual-cursor UI-navigation mode), recording one beat per press into the `InputActivityMeter` that reports every 10 seconds. The meter is constructed before the manager so the wiring exists at start. Guarded by `tests/gamepad.test.ts` (each rising edge is counted exactly once).
- **Static-matrix freeze and scene-root fix (#1254):** Three r165 recomposes and re-multiplies every node's matrices each frame by default, which on this scene is thousands of never-moving prop and terrain nodes paying real per-frame CPU. A new `src/render/static_matrix.ts` freezes a fully-built static subtree (one final `updateMatrixWorld`, then `matrixAutoUpdate` off), applied to the terrain group, the water meshes, and the props tree (1252 nodes frozen; the transform-animated campfire flames are re-enabled explicitly). The scene root is frozen to identity so frozen subtrees skip the world-matrix multiply too, not just the compose, and hidden (distance-culled, off-screen, or compile-gated) view groups set `matrixWorldAutoUpdate` false to skip their 30 to 60 node subtrees, re-opening the gate in the same sync before render so nothing renders stale. `pick()` skips hits on hidden views, and light-owning quest-object glow views are exempt from the gate. Measured in a 30-bot crowd: `multiplyMatrices` self-time 6.35% to 4.76%, combined matrix share 14.6% to 12.7%, fps 79.9 to 82.5; on a north-village walk `updateMatrixWorld` self-share fell from about 10% to 7%.
- **No more first-open icon freeze (#1254):** The first vendor, bags, or loot open used to pay a synchronous 60 to 300ms burst composing every visible item icon (canvas plus `toDataURL`). A new idle-time warmer (`src/ui/icon_prewarm.ts`) drains the whole item and ability catalog through `requestIdleCallback` slices right after boot, so the shared `urlCache` is hot before any window opens. The pump enforces a 6ms wall-clock budget checked before every icon (previously it checked between batches of 8, which could overrun by 60 to 100ms).
- **20 Hz aura decode churn cut (#1254):** `ClientWorld.applyWire` gains an aura decode fast path: when the wire aura ids line up index-for-index with the existing records (the common case, only remaining ticks counting down), it updates the records in place instead of allocating a fresh array plus an object per aura per entity at 20 Hz. Composition changes still rebuild, and preserved object identity matches the offline `Sim` contract. Pinned by snapshot tests covering identity reuse, field updates, rebuild on gain or shrink, same-length reorder, and optional-field clearing.
- **Per-frame work and GC churn trimmed across the client (#1254):** render collapses six per-entity aura `.some()` scans into one pass, reuses the entity's computed `d2` for the loot-sparkle cull, caches the selection-ring terrain drape by (x, z, scale) so a stationary target skips the per-vertex `groundHeight` resample, and adds a per-school projectile color cache that removes three `THREE.Color` allocations per bolt launch (keyed on `GFX.composer` so a tier flip rebuilds the HDR variants). Input gates the hover-cursor scene raycast behind a `HoverPickGate` (re-pick on pointer move, otherwise at most every 50ms), with the cursor kind still re-resolved per frame from live entity state. Net reuses the `applySnapshot` seen-set and updates the threat and cooldown Maps in place, removing per-entity Map allocations at 20 Hz. The HUD early-returns the compass when facing is unchanged, drops the combo-pip array spread for an indexed walk, and reuses the tooltip box size measured at show time so a mousemove no longer forces a reflow per event. The offline sim's UTC-day string is cached in `src/game/utc_day.ts` (recomputed at most once per second), and the sim skips the `pendingLootRolls` defensive copy on empty ticks.
- All #1254 work is behavior-preserving and visually identical: the changes shed only redundant CPU and GC, never actionable information, so the graphics-settings fairness invariant is untouched. Verified green against the parity gate (golden traces plus rng draw order), the architecture and HUD perf-budget guards, and the snapshots and bandwidth guards.

## Social and Chat

- **Whisper collector tab (#1313):** The chat "+" menu now offers a "Whisper" tab alongside the send-capable channels. It is filter-only (whispers target a specific player and have no standing send channel, so it never appears in `CHAT_TAB_CHANNELS` and never runs a `/join`), and it gathers every whisper you send or receive (all carrying channel `whisper`) in one place, away from the busy All view. Opening it from the menu focuses it so the effect is visible, while opening a real channel tab still leaves focus put so it cannot hijack where your typed text goes.
- **Reply from the whisper tab (#1313):** Plain text typed in the Whisper tab replies to whoever last whispered you via `/r` (the new pure `composeWhisperReply` in `src/ui/chat_channels.ts`), so reading and answering both happen from that one tab. An explicit slash command still wins, so "/w Bob hi" from the Whisper tab whispers Bob directly, exactly like a channel tab. With no one to reply to, the sim surfaces its existing "no one has whispered you recently" notice. The tab reuses the existing translated "Whisper" label (`hud.chat.context.whisper`), so no new i18n keys were added, and it persists across sessions (the chat-tab parse and serialize helpers widened from `ChatTabChannel` to `ChatOpenTab`, with parsing still dropping unknown or malformed entries).
- **Whisper from the portrait menu (#1313):** The target and portrait right-click menu gained a "Whisper" action for any player other than yourself (gated on `pid !== playerId`), sitting alongside view profile, invite, trade, and duel. It runs the shared `startWhisper` flow, which prefills "/w <name> " into the chat input and focuses it.
- **Dev whisper bot (#1313):** A new dev-only "/dev bot <name>" (gated by `devCommands`, offline local play only) spawns a stationary, whisperable test player so whispers can be exercised without a second client. The bot is a mage placed 3 yards off your position, marked `isDevBot` (runtime-only, never serialized), and a whisper to it auto-replies, landing back in your chat and Whisper tab and retargeting your `/r`. The recipient's copy of the whisper is skipped for a bot, so the single local client does not render a duplicate of the sender's own line. It is backed by a new append-only `SimContext.spawnDevBot` seam (kept on `Sim`, returns -1 if the name is blank or already taken), so sim purity, the drift guard, and parity stay green.
- **Dev help now lists the bot (#1313):** The bare "/dev" usage fallback string had silently drifted behind the parser and omitted the new command, so it now advertises "/dev bot name" alongside the other cheats (level, tp, give, quest, quests). A new test pins that fallback to list every dev subcommand keyword (level, tp, give, quest, quests, bot) so the usage line can never fall behind the commands again, symmetric to the overhead-emote id-set drift guard.
- **Quest dialog auto-closes when you leave (#1314):** The quest and gossip dialog now closes once you step out of talking range (distance greater than 8 yards from the NPC, or the NPC is gone), matching how the vendor and loot windows already auto-close, instead of lingering until you click the X. You open a dialog within `INTERACT_RANGE` (5 yards), so the wider 8-yard threshold never fires on open.
- **NPC voice fades by distance (#1314):** An NPC's voice line now attenuates by distance while it plays: full volume within 6 yards (`VOICE_FULL_DIST`), ramping linearly to silence by 22 yards (`VOICE_SILENT_DIST`), via a new pure `voiceDistanceGain()` curve in `src/game/voice.ts`. It runs per-frame from `Hud.update()` for a smooth ramp and is independent of the dialog window (which closes at 8 yards), so a line you walk away from keeps fading until the clip ends or you are out of earshot. The distance gain is a separate multiplier layered on top of the settings volume and the per-line gain (a new `setDistanceGain`), leaving both intact, and an overheard voiced yell clears the tracked NPC so the dialogue-distance fade never attenuates it.

## Audio and Content

- Every non-Eastbrook music cue in `src/game/music.ts` was recomposed from scratch around per-place leitmotifs grown from each zone's look and lore, trading the old short loops for multi-section forms scheduled by the existing lookahead timer (#1291). The three Eastbrook Vale themes (`town_eastbrook`, `vale`, `vale_legacy`) are kept note-for-note identical and are now pinned by a sha256 checksum guard in `tests/music.test.ts` so their note data can never drift by accident, and the intro and dungeon-boss mp3 players are left untouched.
- The overworld and town cues were rebuilt as named pieces: Mirefen's "The Water Remembers" (E aeolian, 76 bpm, ABA' over 24 bars, a waterlogged requiem of piano droplets, a drowned choir, and a reed dirge the flute answers from the relative major), Fenbridge's "Dry Boots and Lamplight" (G major, 88 bpm, a 12/8 hearth lilt written as the relative major of the marsh so the town-gate crossfade stays kin), Thornpeak's "The Mountain Listens" (D major, 100 bpm, an anthem to awe to anthem arc with a B minor middle), and Highwatch's "Two Hundred Years of Watch" (B minor, 96 bpm, an eight-bar horn march, an eight-bar pipe descant, and a four-bar chapel-bell coda).
- The three dungeon crawls each received a dedicated theme: Hollow Crypt's "Sleep, Neighbors" (D minor over a phrygian creep, 100 bpm, a funeral bell over an unmoving pedal), Sunken Bastion's "The Drowning Dark" (E minor passacaglia, 116 bpm, a four-note lament ground bass under water textures piling on in four-bar tides), and Gravewyrm Sanctum's "It Breathes Below" (B phrygian, 126 bpm, heartbeat war-drums under an antiphonal cult chant).
- Thornpeak Heights' overworld now plays the dedicated `peaks` anthem instead of borrowing the legacy Vale wilderness cue: the `ZONE_MUSIC` `vale_legacy` override was removed (`ZONE_MUSIC` is now empty, so every zone plays its biome theme), the Thornpeak hub still plays the Highwatch town theme, and `vale_legacy` remains available as a layer but is no longer routed anywhere. The world-music selection test was updated to expect `peaks`.
- The battle cue was reworked into "Vanguard", an orchestral upgrade grown from the original combat cue's own DNA (the pounding D staccato eighth cell `[0,0,3,0,7,0,3,5]`, the timpani one, three, and-of-four pickup skeleton, and bare horn fifths) rather than a drum-kit song. Its first four bars are the original texture over a new bass shadow, then it terraces in octave agitato, war drums, and a rising-fourth war call, takes a half-step shock answered by low brass, marches bVI to bVII home, hits a three-bar tutti, and ends on a drop bar that strikes once, breathes for two beats, and relaunches the cell so chain pulls never hear a dead seam. `COMBAT_TRANSPOSE` was recalibrated to move the D-rooted cue onto each zone's tonal center. Three audition-only variants (`combat_classic`, `combat_wardrums`, `combat_steel`) were composed for the maintainer to pick from and then removed before release, so only the `combat` layer ships.
- BGM loudness was equalized with a measured per-theme trim table (`THEME_TRIM`). The Eastbrook town theme had been playing about 10 dB louder than the quietest cues (Fenbridge, vale), turning zone borders into volume cliffs. Each cue was rendered offline through the exact in-game chain and its gated windowed RMS computed (400 ms windows, windows more than 15 dB under the loudest gated out), then trimmed to the Eastbrook reference; after trims every cue measures within 0.6 dB of target. `MusicDirector.update` drives each layer's gain at `target * trim`, so crossfade behavior is unchanged and the composition data (including Eastbrook) is untouched: this is a mixing change only. New tests require an explicit trim for every registered theme and assert the layer gains are driven through the trim, not a bare 0 or 1.
- The marsh and Fenbridge voices were brought up to the Eastbrook timbre standard without touching their note data: both leads moved off the raw square-wave reed and near-sine pipe onto the Eastbrook flute at Eastbrook velocity, with a harp and hammered dulcimer for attack color, the piano voice was upgraded in place with a stretched inharmonic partial stack (7 partials) so exposed droplets shimmer, and a new detuned-saw oboe voice was added but kept unrouted. Five new synth voices land overall: piano, shaker, brassStab, cymSwell, and oboe.
- Two dev-only auditioning tools shipped with the score. `scripts/render_music.mjs` plus `scripts/music_render_entry.ts` render any theme to a WAV through an `OfflineAudioContext` in headless Chrome with the exact in-game mix chain (master, compressor, hall reverb, and per-theme trim). `music_editor.html` is an in-browser piano-roll editor served by the vite dev server at `/music_editor.html`: it loads the real `buildMusicThemes()` note data, plays it through the real synth and mix chain, and saves edits through a dev-only vite middleware (`POST /__music_editor/save`) into `src/game/music_overrides.generated.ts`, which `buildMusicThemes()` merges over the composed themes. Both the editor page and the save endpoint exist only under the dev server's `configureServer`, never in a production build.
- The `MusicDirector` `localStorage` read (the `ev_music_on` on/off flag) was hardened to fall back cleanly in sandboxed documents where the property access itself throws.

## Architecture and CI

- The IWorld seam is kept a genuine type-only sim import. `src/world_api/chat.ts` previously value-imported `OVERHEAD_EMOTE_IDS` from `sim/types` to back its `isOverheadEmoteId` guard, dragging the deterministic engine into the seam that is supposed to sit above `sim/` and pull it in for types only. It now derives its runtime id set (`OVERHEAD_EMOTE_ID_SET`, a `Set` built from the facet's own `OVERHEAD_EMOTES` list) so the sim import becomes `import type` with no behavior change (#1313, #1314).
- The same change fixes the seam-purity guard itself: `tests/architecture.test.ts` keyed its `SANCTIONED_VALUE_SIM_IMPORTS` allowlist by forward-slash path but looked it up with `path.relative()`'s OS-native separators, so it silently never matched on Windows. A new `posixRel()` normalizer restores the match, and because chat.ts no longer needs an exemption the allowlist is now empty (kept as the escape valve for a future legitimate case).
- Decoupling chat.ts's id set from `sim/types` is only safe while the two lists stay in lockstep, so an id added to `OVERHEAD_EMOTE_IDS` alone would silently fall out of `isOverheadEmoteId` and the emote wheel with no failure. Two drift guards now lock them together: a compile-time type-level equality assertion that reddens `tsc` (the `satisfies` clause in chat.ts already proved there are no extra ids; this proves there are no missing ones), plus runtime set-equality and an `isOverheadEmoteId` accept/reject pass in `tests/overhead_emote_parity.test.ts` and `tests/world_api_parity.test.ts`.
- The `/dev` help fallback is pinned to every subcommand. The usage line had drifted behind the parser (it omitted `/dev bot`), so a new test in `tests/chat.test.ts` asserts a bare `/dev` advertises each of level, tp, give, quest, quests, and bot, symmetric to the overhead-emote drift guard so the help can never fall behind the commands again.
- The non-blocking AI PR reviewer can now be re-run on demand (#1222). An OWNER, MEMBER, or COLLABORATOR commenting `/review` or `/suggest <focus>` on a pull request fires a new `ai-review-comment` job in `.github/workflows/pr-ai.yml`, gated on the commenter's `author_association` evaluated against the base repo (so a fork PR's own author cannot self-trigger it). The job never checks out the PR head: `scripts/ai_review.mjs` fetches the diff as text through the GitHub REST API diff media type, so no untrusted code runs with repo secrets present, and a comment-triggered review posts as its own reply keyed to the triggering comment id instead of overwriting the standing sticky review. A `/suggest` focus string is threaded into the model prompt, and the default `OPENROUTER_MODEL` moved off the pulled `openrouter/owl-alpha` to `nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b:free`.
- Adding the `issue_comment` trigger meant the heavy screenshots job (checkout, npm ci, Vite boot, puppeteer) was firing on every PR or issue comment, so it is now gated with `if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'`. The known comment-path limitation, that `listTouchedDirs` sees only the base tree and misses files the PR itself newly adds, is documented in the script.
- Biome format-only cleanup ran over the files this cycle's branches changed (`scripts/pr2_primitives_shots.mjs`, `scripts/talent_primitives_shots.mjs`, `src/sim/combat/effect_dispatch.ts`, `src/sim/sim.ts`, `tests/ground_target_cast.test.ts`, `tests/pvp_safety.test.ts`), formatting and organizing imports with no logic changes so the changed-file `biome ci --changed` gate stays clean.
- The version bumped to 0.19.0. `package.json` and `package-lock.json` move from 0.18.0, and the `scripts/version_sync.mjs` hook cascades to the native manifests: Android `versionName 0.19.0` with `versionCode` 7 to 8, and iOS `MARKETING_VERSION 0.19.0` with `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` 8 to 9.

## Upgrade Notes

**Talents 2.0 and ground targeting (#1305, #1064):**

- Wire changes are additive and backward compatible: a new castAt client command (client proposes a ground point, server clamps it to range) and a new world-anchored spellfxAt event. No character schema or persistence change: Entity.castAim is transient combat state, not persisted CharacterState.
- A new client-local setting groundReticle (Interface, default on) enables the desktop ground-targeting reticle; no operator action required.
- The casting and conditional primitives (#1305) are content-unused this cycle and draw no new rng, so the parity goldens are unmodified. i18n was regenerated for the five new spell names and descriptions and the reticle label.

**Daily Rewards (#1307, #1310):**

- New persistence: PR #1307 adds seven realm-scoped tables to server/db.ts SCHEMA (applied on boot by ensureSchema under a pg_advisory_xact_lock): daily_reward_days (PK day, realm; prize_pool_usd, woc_usd_price, finalized_at, discord_announced_at, created_at), daily_reward_scores (PK day, realm, account_id; points, updated_at; plus index daily_reward_scores_rank), daily_reward_events (BIGSERIAL id PK; kind, points, idempotency_key, meta JSONB; UNIQUE day, realm, account_id, idempotency_key), daily_reward_spins (PK day, realm, account_id; outcome_key, points), daily_reward_tasks (PK day, realm, task_id; task_type, title, description, points, base_points, sort_order, active, config JSONB), daily_reward_task_completions (PK day, realm, account_id, task_id; created but currently has no writer), and daily_reward_payouts (PK day, realm, rank; username, wallet_pubkey, points, prize_percent, prize_usd, status, tx_signature, error, paid_at; plus index daily_reward_payouts_status). All account_id columns reference accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. Two JSONB columns are added: daily_reward_events.meta (default '{}') and daily_reward_tasks.config (default '{}').
- The DDL is additive and idempotent, safe on every boot: all statements are CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS, or ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS (task_type, base_points, config on daily_reward_tasks; discord_announced_at on daily_reward_days), plus one one-shot backfill UPDATE daily_reward_tasks SET base_points = points WHERE base_points = 0 AND points > 0. No columns are dropped or retyped and nothing is destructive; it is a pure schema add over v0.18.0.
- New env/secrets (all optional, feature stays off until set): WOC_DAILY_REWARD_SERVICE_URL (origin of the private payout service that owns runtime config and executes payouts; unset falls back to built-in defaults of 20 USD minimum, 150 USD pool, one quest task), WOC_DAILY_REWARD_SERVICE_SECRET (dedicated shared secret sent to the payout service as x-woc-daily-reward-secret and required to authorize the server's own /internal/daily-rewards/* payout endpoints; internalAuthorized fails closed when unset, so those endpoints stay locked, and it deliberately does NOT fall back to RESTART_COUNTDOWN_SECRET), and WOC_DAILY_REWARD_CONFIG_TTL_MS (config cache TTL, default 300000). docker-compose.yml now passes these three through to the game service.
- Discord winner announcements need DISCORD_DAILY_REWARDS_CHANNEL_ID on the bot (passed through in docker-compose.yml); the bot authenticates to /internal/discord/daily-rewards-winners with the existing DISCORD_BOT_SECRET, so no new bot secret is required. If the channel id is unset the bot logs once and skips the feed.
- Gameplay gating: daily rewards require a linked/verified wallet holding at least the configured USD minimum of WOC. There is no client-authoritative point crediting; all points are credited server-side (quest turn-in, arena result, online-time sweeps), and the intermediate client-asserted POST /api/daily-rewards/task route was removed before release.

**Desktop and Native (#1289, #1288, #1286):**

- The Electron desktop app is a new distribution surface built from the same Vite client, shipping a website channel and a Steam channel.
- Website auto-update feed: publish a static HTTPS host serving https://updates.worldofclaudecraft.com/desktop/ (electron-builder generic provider) with the release/ installers and update metadata; the app only issues GETs. Steam builds (npm run electron:build:steam) do not use it and ship with the in-app updater off.
- Desktop build stamps: set VITE_DESKTOP_API_ORIGIN (and optional VITE_DESKTOP_LOGIN_ORIGIN) at BUILD time to point the packaged app at the right API and login origins. Both are baked into the bundle and stamped into the packaged package.json (wocDesktop), and installed builds ignore them as runtime env. WOC_DISTRIBUTION and the VITE_DESKTOP_* pair apply to electron:dev only.
- Crash uploads are OFF unless you set a build-time, https-only WOC_CRASH_SUBMIT_URL (for example a Sentry minidump endpoint); otherwise minidumps stay local under the crashDumps path.
- Code signing is opt-in via env: macOS CSC_LINK + CSC_KEY_PASSWORD (or CSC_NAME) plus notarization via APPLE_API_KEY, APPLE_API_KEY_ID, APPLE_API_ISSUER; Windows Azure Artifact Signing via WIN_SIGN_PUBLISHER_NAME, WIN_SIGN_ENDPOINT, WIN_SIGN_ACCOUNT_NAME, WIN_SIGN_PROFILE_NAME (plus AZURE_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID/CLIENT_SECRET for CI auth). Unsigned local builds fall back to ad-hoc signing; macOS auto-update requires a real Developer ID and notarization. Full runbook: docs/desktop-release.md.
- Before any public desktop release, deploy this branch's server: it adds desktop-origin CORS reflection (DESKTOP_APP_ORIGINS in server/web_login_guard.ts), the /desktop-login one-time-code handoff (server/desktop_login.ts), and Turnstile admission for the app:// origin (server/turnstile.ts). Without it, every REST call from an installed app fails CORS. This is additive server code: no database schema or persistence change.
- Android builds now require the com.google.android.play:app-update:2.1.0 dependency (added to android/app/build.gradle), and both native projects register a new NativeAppUpdate Capacitor plugin. The update prompt introduces no save, wire, or schema change; its 24-hour snooze uses client localStorage keys only (woc.nativeUpdate.*). Appflow OTA is now disabled (capacitor.config.ts autoUpdateMethod: none), so native builds update through the app stores only.

**UI, rendering, and audio (#1284, #1202, #1311, #1254, #1291):**

- Client-only cycle: all four UI/render/input workstreams touch render, ui, input, and settings. No schema, persistence, or wire-format changes, and no new environment variables. The aura decode fast path is backward compatible and preserves the existing wire shape.
- New static assets ship with the client bundle: 101 webp icons plus mapping.json under public/ui/items/. This is additive and safe; pull them as-is.
- The home graphics selector reuses the existing graphicsPreset setting (localStorage woc_settings); no migration is needed and no gameplay value is affected (presets are cosmetic-only per the graphics-settings fairness invariant).
- The soundtrack stays pure WebAudio synthesis with no new audio assets; the only new tracked source file is src/game/music_overrides.generated.ts (currently empty), which buildMusicThemes() merges over the composed themes at runtime. The music editor page (/music_editor.html) and its save endpoint (POST /__music_editor/save) are registered only under the vite dev server's configureServer and are never included in a production build.

**Localization, native versions, and CI (#1222):**

- The diff includes regenerated i18n artifacts (resolved translation tables, i18n.status.summary.json, and i18n.resolved.sha256); pull them as-is rather than regenerating by hand.
- The native version bumped to 0.19.0: Android versionCode advanced 7 to 8 and iOS CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION advanced 8 to 9. Nothing here changes database schema or persistence.
- The default AI-reviewer model moved off openrouter/owl-alpha to nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b:free; override it with the OPENROUTER_MODEL repo variable. The on-demand /review and /suggest path still no-ops without the OPENROUTER_API_KEY repo secret and only triggers for OWNER, MEMBER, or COLLABORATOR comments.

## Size

336 files changed, 25,353 insertions, 923 deletions versus v0.18.0. Major changed areas: the Talents 2.0 casting primitives and ground-targeted casting across sim, render, and ui; wallet-gated Daily Rewards across server, net, and ui with seven new realm-scoped tables and Discord winner announcements; the new Electron desktop app under electron/ plus the native store-update prompt and the Appflow OTA cutover; the client hot-path performance pass; the from-scratch soundtrack recomposition and loudness equalization; the whisper quality-of-life and NPC voice work; the IWorld seam and CI hardening; and the release localization fill.

## Major PRs and Workstreams Included

- #1322 Release v0.19.0
- #1305 Talents 2.0 casting and conditional primitives
- #1064 Ground-targeted casting
- #1307 Wallet-gated daily rewards with Discord winner announcements
- #1310 Configurable reward day start
- #1289 Electron desktop app (website and Steam channels, auto-update, crash capture)
- #1288 Native store-update prompt
- #1286 Disable Appflow OTA updates
- #1284 Home graphics selector
- #1202 Resource item icons and hunter pet-bar icon fix
- #1311 Gamepad button presses count toward APM
- #1254 Client hot-path performance pass
- #1291 Soundtrack recomposition and loudness equalization
- #1313 Whisper tab, portrait whisper action, and dev whisper bot
- #1314 Quest dialog auto-close and distance-faded NPC voice
- #1222 On-demand AI reviewer via /review and /suggest

## Contributors

Thanks to the contributors represented in the release range: Ryan Foo, Fernando Ramirez, Trev Cavill, Enrique Garcia-Ferrer, Blaine, Reuben Horne, Donny-Deals, Matthieu Huguet, Max Polaczuk.
