patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.18.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.18.0  
**Date:** 2026-07-02  
**Previous release:** v0.17.0  

v0.18.0 is a large gameplay and platform release with **65 commits** since v0.17.0. The headline is a sweeping talent tooltip accuracy pass and buff rebalance that makes every talent across all 9 classes and 27 specs state its real numbers, alongside a new developer badges system that rewards contributors who land commits in the open-source repo. This cycle also delivers party and raid loot settings with master looter, a World Market expansion with realm-scoping, Discord guild auto-join, and a broad stability and infrastructure hardening pass.

## Highlights

- **Talents:** Every talent tooltip across all 9 classes and 27 specs now spells out the exact numbers its effect produces, buff-strengthening talents that previously did nothing now deliver their promised bonuses, and caster and raid buffs are rebalanced into percentage, party-wide values (#1039, #1038).
- **Buff tooltips online:** Buff and debuff hover tooltips now show real magnitudes online, so Aspect of the Hawk reads "Increases attack power by 20" instead of "by 0" and percent effects, DoTs, and absorbs all display their true values (#1220).
- **Developer badges:** Contributors who land commits in the open-source repo now earn cosmetic developer badges through a verified GitHub link, with five tiers, nameplate and inspect flair, and a new Developers leaderboard tab (#1086).
- **Loot and raid:** A new party and raid Loot Settings window surfaces the group's loot rules to everyone, and whole-group loot messaging broadcasts the full loot lifecycle with clickable item links (#1216).
- **World Market:** The World Market is now realm-scoped so realms stop clobbering each other's listings (#946), with a critical fix that preserves listings whose item id is no longer known (#947).
- **Discord:** Linking or logging in with Discord auto-joins the World of ClaudeCraft guild via guilds.join, so players land in the community server the moment they connect (#1192, #1204).
- **Items:** Gear now requires a minimum level to equip (#1020) and all potions share a global 2-minute cooldown shown on the action bar (#1024).
- **Server stability:** The server now terminates sessions on unbounded send backpressure (#950) and isolates each session in the broadcast, event, and tick hot loops (#949), so one stuck or faulty client can no longer OOM the process or starve every other player of snapshots.

## Combat and Talents

- Every talent tooltip across all 9 classes and 27 specs now spells out the real numbers its effect produces (#1039). A gate test (tests/talent_tooltip_accuracy.test.ts) enforces that a tooltip is never vague, never states a number the effect does not produce, and lists every effect magnitude. 85 vague tooltips now give per-rank numbers, and 5 incomplete tooltips (Guardian Favor, Arcane Focus, Improved Devotion Aura, Aspect of the Hawk, Fortitude) now describe every value.
- Buff-strengthening talents that previously did nothing now work: Improved Devotion Aura, Aspect of the Hawk, and Fortitude carried a damage modifier on pure buff abilities (which do no damage), so a new buffPct ability-mod now scales the buff itself, delivering the promised +20% armor, attack power, or stamina.
- Three effect and tooltip mismatches are reconciled: Lightning Reflexes now grants +10% Agility, Arcane Mind +8% Intellect, and Emberstorm is tuned from 12% to 10%. Barrage now cuts Concussive Shot's cooldown instead of buffing its negligible damage.
- Caster and raid buffs are rebalanced into percentage, party-wide values (#1038): Mark of the Wild (+5% all attributes), Arcane Intellect (+5% Intellect), Power Word: Fortitude (+5% Stamina), Blessing of Might (+10% attack power), Devotion Aura (+10% armor), and Battle Shout (+10% attack power). Battle Shout and Devotion Aura keep their exclusive-group mutual exclusivity.
- Spell Power now scales healing, not just damage: direct heals take the cast-time coefficient and HoTs take the per-tick coefficient, so healers can gear their output. A double-dip guard keeps a HoT that rides a direct heal (Regrowth) from scaling twice, and determinism is preserved (the rider consumes no rng draws).
- Armor debuffs are now non-stacking percentages combined by maximum: Sunder Armor at 2% per stack (10% at 5) and Faerie Fire at a flat 10%. Thorns scales with the druid's Spell Power, snapshot at cast. The shadow priest is retuned: SW:Pain, Mind Blast, and Mind Flay damage up, Mind Blast cooldown from 8s to 6s, and Mind Flay cost from 45 to 40.
- The character sheet gains a per-stat source breakdown, including the new Spell Power stat: hovering a stat shows a "Made up of" list of its upstream sources (Base, from an attribute, gear, buffs, and talents) so players can see exactly where each point comes from.
- Online buff and debuff hover tooltips now show real magnitudes (#1220). The server previously stripped an aura's value for everything except negative stat-saps, so positive buffs like Aspect of the Hawk read "Increases attack power by 0", percent effects showed a garbage "100%", and DoTs and absorbs read 0. The wire now carries value, value2, value3, tickInterval, and school for every value-bearing aura, and the fix also derives the unit-frame shield overlay online. It is backward compatible and changes only the server-encode and ClientWorld-decode seam.
- The classic threat pull-over boundary now respects a mob's actual size (#987). A challenger still pulls aggro above 110% of the tank's threat in melee or above 130% at range, but "in melee" was a flat 6 yards, which misclassified a player standing at a large boss's feet as ranged and wrongly required 130%. The boundary now uses the mob's size-scaled reach.
- The taunt and forced-target window now keeps counting down while a mob is stunned, so a stun no longer freezes the timer and unexpectedly extends how long a taunt holds.
- The Gravecaller Summoner (Zone 2 trash) loses its Wail of the Grave fear proc (#995). Mob-sourced crowd control bypasses diminishing returns, so the summoner stacked a full 4s silence and a full 4s fear on one victim with no safety valve. It keeps Silencing Shriek (4s silence) and Grave Blight (heal absorb), remaining a dangerous anti-caster without the double crowd control.

## Developer Badges

- Contributors who land commits in the open-source repo now earn cosmetic developer badges (#1086), mirroring the existing token-holder and Discord status-tier systems. The badges are purely cosmetic: the simulation never reads them.
- Players link a verified GitHub account through GitHub OAuth using a "Link GitHub" control beside the wallet-link row on character select, following the same flow as the existing Discord link.
- Landed-commit counts come from GitHub's public contributor stats for the repo, cached server-side with a 5-minute backoff after a failed fetch so a rate-limited API is not hammered.
- There are five rungs by landed commits: Tinkerer (1+), Artificer (10+), Runesmith (50+), Architect (150+), and Worldwright (500+), each with its own procedural "commit graph" SVG badge distinct from the holder coins and Discord numerals.
- Badges surface across the game: a glyph next to other players' nameplates (with a glowing name outline for Architect and above, composed on top of any Discord staff color), the inspect screen (badge, tier name, commit count, and @login), and the shareable player card PNG.
- The high-score window gains a third "Developers" tab that ranks contributors by landed commits alongside the existing Players and Guilds boards.
- Operators get telemetry: the contributor-stats cache and the link flow report fetch, hit, miss, failure, and link request and failure counts through the same admin provider-usage view as the token-balance and releases proxies.

## Loot and Raid

- **Loot Settings window (#1216):** A closable, reopenable window shows the group's loot rules. The leader edits the loot method (group loot or master looter) and the roll threshold, while members get a read-only view. It opens from the group-panel right-click menu and auto-opens when you become party leader, so a new group's loot rules are front and center. The window docks below the party frames and is draggable, and it never steals keyboard focus or closes your other windows.
- **Whole-group loot messaging (#1216):** The entire party or raid now sees setting changes (stating exactly what changed), a loot-settings summary sent to a joining member, the effective master looter changing on leave or promote, a re-announce on party to and from raid conversion, and the full loot lifecycle (rolling, won, assigned, passed) for premium roll-worthy items, each with a clickable item link.
- **Master looter curate window:** The master looter gets a 5-minute window to curate loot before it is distributed.
- **Promote to leader:** A new promote-to-leader social action lets you hand off group leadership, and master loot follows the handoff so the new leader controls distribution.
- **Raid lockout reset (#1031):** Raid lockouts are standardized to a single daily reset on us-east-1 time, so every raid shares one predictable reset schedule.

## Items

- **Minimum level to equip (#1020):** Gear now requires a minimum level to equip, derived from the item's power tier so higher-tier gear cannot be worn early.
- **Shared potion cooldown on the action bar (#1024):** All potions share one global 2-minute cooldown (the classic-era combat-potion value), now surfaced as a cooldown swipe and countdown on potion action-bar slots so players can see the shared timer at a glance. Quaffing one potion puts every potion slot on the same swipe.
- **Sell food and drink in stacks (#1022):** Food and drink can be sold in stacks of 5 at classic per-unit pricing.
- **Shift-click item links (#1027):** Shift-clicking an item inserts a clickable item link into chat, and this link infrastructure is reused by the new whole-group loot messaging.

## World Market and Economy

- **Second auctioneer and full market browsing:** Highwatch gains a second auctioneer, and the whole market is now paginated and filterable so large listing sets are navigable.
- **Realm-scoped World Market (#946):** The World Market is scoped per realm (keyed as market:<realm>) so separate realms no longer clobber each other's listings. The leave-path market write and a one-shot legacy row migration are aligned to the same realm scoping.
- **Keep listings for unknown item ids (#947):** A CRITICAL data-loss fix keeps market listings whose item id is no longer known, so listings are no longer dropped when an item id becomes unrecognized.
- **Market dropdown and mobile fixes:** Market filter dropdowns are repositioned to stay inside the clip box, a dead container lookup and border-box overcount in dropdown placement are fixed, and mobile market scrolling is improved.

## Discord

- Linking or logging in with Discord now auto-joins the World of ClaudeCraft guild via the Discord guilds.join flow, so players land in the community server the moment they connect their account (#1192).
- The game service now receives DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, giving the server the credential it needs to perform the guild auto-join on link and login (#1204).
- Broken Discord avatar images are now hidden when the Discord CDN fails to load, so a failed request no longer leaves a broken-image icon in the UI (#1186).
- When a Discord avatar fails to load, each surface falls back to its own native no-avatar state rather than a single generic placeholder, keeping the fallback consistent with where it appears (#1188).
- The Discord menu is now a rebindable keybind, defaulting to U, so players can move or clear it like any other binding (#1185).

## Moderation and Admin

- In-game moderation commands now specify character names, so moderators act on an explicit target instead of relying on ambiguous context (#1187).
- The admin IP association page can now open the account details modal directly, letting operators jump from a shared-IP cluster straight into a specific account (#1087).

## HUD and UI

- A new Auto-Attack on Ability Use option (Options, Interface, on by default) engages your white-swing auto-attack when you use an offensive ability, so a separate Attack press is no longer required. It only fires on abilities that deal damage and apply no damage-breakable crowd control, so incapacitating strikes, heals, buffs, and pure crowd control never start a swing (#1063).

## Content

- Dungeons and delves are now reported as their own zone in presence and /who, so players inside instanced content show the correct location instead of the surrounding open-world zone (#988).
- The Gravewyrm Sanctum approach is now clear: the Boneclad Revenant gate pack and a clipping Wyrmcult zealot pack are moved off the x=0 approach road so players can walk to the gate without being aggroed on entry or exit. Spawn counts are unchanged, so world-gen remains byte-identical elsewhere (#1025).
- Added the professions system high-level design document, capturing the planned direction for professions ahead of implementation (#1168).

## Native and Mobile

- The mobile touch More menu now includes a Discord link and unlink entry, bringing account linking to touch players who cannot use the desktop keybind (#1184).
- The iOS release now requires full screen, meeting the platform presentation requirement for the native build (#1100).

## Server and Networking

- The game server now terminates any WebSocket session whose outbound buffer grows without bound, guarding against a single non-draining client (a frozen tab, a wedged proxy, or a deliberately non-reading peer) accumulating an unbounded send backlog and running the whole realm process out of memory (#950).
- Backpressure is enforced with a hard 8 MiB outbound-buffer limit in a pure, unit-tested helper (server/ws_backpressure.ts); a session over the limit is torn down through the existing close and leave path so the player is fully removed and can rejoin cleanly (#950).
- Each session is now isolated inside the broadcast, event-routing, and tick hot loops, so a throw while building or routing state for one player degrades to a single skipped session instead of starving every other player of snapshots and events that tick (#949).
- Per-session and per-tick bodies run through a host-agnostic guard (server/guarded_iter.ts) as defense in depth: a persistent fault is contained to one bad session, a transient fault still self-heals on the next tick, and the serialized snapshot and event payloads stay byte-identical (#949).

## Tooling and CI

- A unified game performance profiler ships as a CLI plus a reusable core, giving operators and contributors one consistent way to profile the game (#1014).
- Continuous integration now takes change-aware screenshots, shooting only the screens the diff actually touches, alongside a non-blocking PR AI assist reviewer whose prompt is sharpened and fed repo context (#1036).
- The PR AI reviewer script (ai_review.mjs) no longer builds shell commands from untrusted touched-directory input, closing a shell-injection path in listTouchedDirs.
- New FBX to GLB conversion scripts standardize art assets into the renderer's preferred model format (#1089).
- The release malware scan now skips .claude/worktrees copies so duplicated worktree files no longer trip the release gate (#1084).
- docker-compose now passes the GitHub OAuth environment through to the game service so OAuth-linked features work in the composed deployment (#1225).

## Localization

- The v0.18.0 release localization fill translates every non-English string added this cycle, covering the new developer badge, loot, market, Discord, and stat strings, so no player-visible text falls back to English at release.
- The fill covers all 17 base locales with dialect locales inheriting from their base, and it regenerates the resolved translation tables and hash baseline so the release-tier i18n gate passes clean.

## Validation

Release PR #1226 (release/v0.18.0 into main) merged 2026-07-01 as commit 04c4338a, which is this release's tag target. The following gates were verified green before merge:

- tsc plus admin svelte-check clean.
- Full test suite at RELEASE tier: 6354 passed, 3 skipped.
- biome ci clean on changed files.
- I18N_RELEASE_TIER=1 gate green (all locales filled).
- docker compose build and up smoke-tested.

The previous baseline is v0.17.0.

## Upgrade Notes

- docker-compose now passes GitHub OAuth and other server environment through to the game service. Set GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and GITHUB_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET to enable the new developer badges link flow.
- Set DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN on the game server to enable Discord guild auto-join on link and login.
- The diff includes regenerated i18n artifacts (resolved translation tables and hash baseline); pull them as-is rather than regenerating by hand.
- There are no destructive schema changes.
- The World Market realm-scoping migration is a global one-shot and safe: it aligns legacy rows to the market:<realm> keying without data loss.

## Size

380 files changed, 24,415 insertions, 5,986 deletions versus v0.17.0. Major changed areas: the sim content and talents tables (tooltip accuracy and buff rebalance), the developer badges system across net, server, and UI, the loot settings and whole-group loot messaging, the World Market realm-scoping and browsing, Discord auto-join and avatar fallbacks, server backpressure and hot-loop isolation, and the release localization fill.

## Major PRs and Workstreams Included

- #1226 Release v0.18.0
- #1039 Talent tooltip accuracy across all classes and specs
- #1038 Caster and raid buff rebalance
- #1220 Online buff and debuff tooltip magnitudes
- #987 Threat pull-over boundary respects mob size
- #995 Gravecaller Summoner fear proc removal
- #1086 Developer badges
- #1216 Loot Settings window and whole-group loot messaging
- #1031 Raid lockout single daily reset
- #1020 Minimum level to equip
- #1024 Shared potion cooldown on the action bar
- #1022 Sell food and drink in stacks
- #1027 Shift-click item links
- #946 Realm-scoped World Market
- #947 Keep listings for unknown item ids
- #1192 Discord guild auto-join
- #1204 DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN on the game server
- #1186 Hide broken Discord avatars
- #1188 Per-surface Discord avatar fallbacks
- #1185 Rebindable Discord menu keybind
- #1187 Moderation commands specify character names
- #1087 Admin IP association account modal
- #1063 Auto-Attack on Ability Use
- #988 Dungeons and delves report as their own zone
- #1025 Gravewyrm Sanctum approach clearing
- #1168 Professions system design document
- #1184 Mobile Discord link and unlink entry
- #1100 iOS full-screen requirement
- #950 WebSocket send backpressure
- #949 Per-session hot-loop isolation
- #1014 Unified game performance profiler
- #1036 Change-aware CI screenshots and PR AI reviewer
- #1089 FBX to GLB conversion scripts
- #1084 Malware scan skips worktree copies
- #1225 docker-compose passes GitHub OAuth through

## Contributors

Thanks to the contributors represented in the release range: jgyy, Reuben Horne, Fernando Ramirez, Nervescraper, Ryan Foo, Enrique Garcia-Ferrer, Trev Cavill, Matthieu Huguet, Donny-Deals, awidearray, and Alejandro Aznar.

patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.17.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.17.0  
**Date:** 2026-07-01  
**Previous release:** v0.16.0

v0.17.0 is a Discord integration, public guide/wiki, social, admin moderation, and stability release with **117 commits** since v0.16.0. The headline is two-way Discord integration: account linking with a first-login account choice and safe unlink, in-game rewards and flair, and a relay bot wired into the Docker/compose deployment. It also ships a much richer public guide (pre-rendered monster and class still images, a 3D model viewer and gallery, and a Delves page), a guild high-score board, the ability to inspect another player's equipped gear, live admin moderation and suspicious-player review tooling, a single-action-bar default, and a broad set of combat, pet, talent, camera, mobile, rendering-stability, and localization fixes.

## Highlights

- **Discord integration:** Two-way Discord support with account linking, a first-login account choice, safe unlink, in-game rewards and flair, and a relay bot, with the bot built and run as part of the Docker/compose stack.
- **Public guide and wiki:** Pre-rendered monster and class still images, a working 3D model viewer and model gallery, a new Delves page, bestiary coverage for temple and delve creatures, and wiki prose brought in line with the current game systems.
- **Leaderboards and inspect:** Added a guild high-score board alongside the player leaderboard, and the ability to inspect another player's equipped gear.
- **Admin and moderation:** Enriched online player positions with human-friendly locations, a live suspicious-player review dashboard, in-game moderation commands with a spectate mode and separated /ban and /suspend, and sortable shared IPs.
- **HUD and player UX:** The second action bar is now opt-in (single bar by default), the World Market scrolls on mobile and uses a wider window, nameplate clicks reliably target the unit (so party/healer clicks work), and the leaderboard tabs use the proper tablist pattern.
- **Stability:** Fixed the "Could not start the renderer" entry crashes via tier-independent asset preloading and the missing play.html action-bar element, plus camera-snap, native preview-context, and mobile landscape menu fixes.

## Discord Integration

- Added two-way Discord integration covering account login/linking, in-game rewards, an in-game flair indicator, and a relay bot.
- Added a first-login account choice flow so a Discord login can attach to an existing account or create a new one, with safe unlink handling for linked accounts.
- Made the in-game link-Discord call-to-action banner responsive on phones, with the chooser divider centered between the buttons.
- Wired the Discord bot into the container stack: it now runs in compose, its build script is included in the Docker build context, and it boots without requiring a dotenv file.

## Public Guide and Wiki

- Added build-time pre-rendering of monster and class still images, shown in the bestiary, class, and model gallery pages.
- Added a 3D model viewer for creature and class models, an auto-rotating class hero model on the classes index, and a model gallery turntable framed from posed bounds.
- Added a Delves page and derived the wiki delve roster from the sim DELVE registry, and surfaced temple creatures and delve data in the bestiary generator.
- Brought wiki prose in line with the current game systems and refreshed reworded blurbs.
- Hardened model viewer reliability: dispose skeletons and clamp the framing radius, release WebGL contexts when destroy races an in-flight load, and free the preview/native context before game entry.
- Added screen-reader status announcements for the inline model viewer and a group role for the gallery picker, plus guide tests for the model embed, viewer wiring, delves, and bestiary spoiler safety.

## Leaderboards, Inspect, and Social

- Added a guild high-score board next to the player leaderboard, with the Players/Guilds tabs using the real tablist keyboard pattern.
- Added the ability to inspect another player's equipped gear.

## Admin, Moderation, and Operations

- Enriched the admin online-players view with human-friendly location labels (zone, dungeon, delve, and nearest landmark) for each online player.
- Added a live suspicious-player review dashboard for operators.
- Added in-game moderation commands and a spectate mode, and separated /ban from /suspend while hardening the in-game command surface.
- Added sorting controls to the admin shared IPs page.

## HUD, UI, and Player UX

- Made the second action bar opt-in, defaulting to a single bar; abilities bound to the second bar's slots stay castable via their keybinds.
- Let the World Market scroll on mobile, widened the World Market window, and removed the dead height cap.
- Fixed nameplate clicks so they target the underlying unit, restoring party/healer click-targeting.
- Committed the final camera yaw on mouselook release to stop the camera/character snap.

## Gameplay, Combat, and Persistence Fixes

- Capped mob-to-player miss so enemy mobs always have at least an 80% chance to hit players, fixing an unintended player advantage.
- Stopped persisting warlock demons across logout.
- Revalidated persisted talent allocations on load so stale or invalid allocations are repaired.
- Notified the owner when a stored pet's saved template no longer exists, and localized that notice.

## Native, Mobile, and Rendering Stability

- Fixed the "Could not start the renderer" entry crashes by preloading prop and character GLBs tier-independently and adding the missing #actionbar2 element to play.html.
- Keyed prop material prewarm off the live graphics tier rather than the import-frozen guess.
- Reworked the landscape native menus and capped graphics quality for mobile devices.

## Localization

- Translated the new guide/wiki prose keys into every supported locale (Romance, Germanic, Nordic, CJK, and Cyrillic groups) and localized the delve keeper and companion format strings.
- Completed the release-gate locale fill for v0.17.0: filled the admin friendly-location keys (location labels, dungeon/delve names, and zone point-of-interest names) and the opt-in second action bar toggle across all locales, reusing the canonical in-game translations for content names.
- Regenerated the resolved game and admin i18n tables, the status summary, and the game-table hash baseline, and cleared the full 14-locale release gate.

## Validation

- Release PR: #1080, `release/v0.17.0` into `main`, merged 2026-07-01.
- Target commit: `b00fb6a5d6d0e1ffab9327ddcbfeb730267ab05e`.
- PR checks passed before merge: changed-file Biome lint, the English-only PR gate, and the full 14-locale release gate.
- Previous release baseline: `v0.16.0`.

## Upgrade Notes

- Deployments now include a Discord bot service. Build the stack with the Discord bot build script in the Docker context, run the bot in compose, and provide the Discord credentials through host-managed environment configuration (the bot boots without a dotenv file present).
- Provision the Discord application/bot credentials and OAuth settings for account linking and rewards before enabling the integration.
- No destructive schema changes are required by this release; deploy with the existing deploy playbook and standard backup step.
- The guide build pre-renders monster and class still images; treat the regenerated guide-still assets as new asset paths for caching/CDN.
- Operators should expect regenerated i18n/admin locale artifacts and the new guide still images in the diff.

## Size

Compared with v0.16.0:

- **347 files changed**
- **33,386 additions / 3,742 deletions**
- Major changed areas: `src/` (guide, ui, render, sim, admin, net), `server/`, `public/` guide stills, `tests/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, Discord bot and Docker/compose packaging, and regenerated localization artifacts.

## Major PRs and Workstreams Included

- #1080 Release v0.17.0
- #1079 Responsive link-Discord CTA banner on phones
- #1078 Rework landscape native menus
- #1076 Enrich admin online player positions with friendly locations
- #1075 Discord account choice on first login and safe unlink
- #1072 Allow the Discord bot to boot without a dotenv file
- #1071 Include the Discord bot build script in the Docker context
- #1069 Run the Discord bot in compose
- #1060 Wiki monster stills, 3D viewer fixes, and a Delves page
- #1059 Guild high-score board
- #1058 World Market mobile scroll
- #1055 Nameplate clicks target the unit
- #1054 Release native preview context before game entry
- #1053 Commit final camera yaw on mouselook release
- #1047 Second action bar opt-in (single bar by default)
- #1046 Fix the "Could not start the renderer" entry crashes
- #1045 Live suspicious-player review dashboard
- #1044 Two-way Discord integration (login, rewards, bot, relay, in-game flair)
- #1029 Inspect another player's equipped gear
- #1026 In-game moderation commands and spectate mode
- #1002 /press coming-soon page
- #973 Notify owner when a stored pet's template no longer exists
- #963 Do not persist warlock demons across logout
- #953 Revalidate persisted talent allocations on load
- #546 Cap mob-to-player miss (enemy mobs always hit players at >=80%)

## Contributors

Thanks to the contributors represented in the release range: Fernando Ramirez, jgyy, Reuben Horne, Trev Cavill, Matthieu Huguet, Enrique Garcia-Ferrer, Ryan Foo, and Max Polaczuk.

patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.16.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.16.0  
**Date:** 2026-06-29  
**Previous release:** v0.15.0

v0.16.0 is a very large gameplay, systems, admin, performance, frontend, localization, native, and architecture release. It includes the `release/v0.16.0` line plus the post-v0.15.0 mainline refactor and modernization work that was merged before this release tag.

The headline player-facing changes are projectile damage on impact, a new Spell Power stat, item levels and item set bonuses, master loot, free-for-all corpse unlocks after a short owner lock, a second action bar, debuff separation, richer aura and item tooltips, cancelable player buffs, WebP class ability icons, major render freeze fixes, crowd LOD, a rebuilt Svelte 5 admin dashboard with moderation tooling, and native OTA/store polish.

The headline engineering changes are the SimContext extraction campaign, IWorld facet split, frontend HUD and CSS modernization, stronger accessibility infrastructure, graphics fairness gates, expanded localization to 21 locale files, full release i18n gates, private bot-detector Docker overlay support, and a much stronger QA/reviewer workflow.

## Highlights

- **Combat timing and spell resolution:** Projectile spells now deal damage on impact instead of on cast, spells are resisted instead of missed, Lightning Shield has a 3-charge cap and internal cooldown, hunter aspects are mutually exclusive, Wolf Form uses the rogue cadence, stun chains have diminishing returns, pets despawn on owner death, and ability/combat-potion cooldowns persist through logout.
- **Itemization and loot:** Added item levels with stat-budget normalization, item set bonuses, armor-type coverage requirements, armor type display in item tooltips, master loot, randomized tied rolls, probability validation, and a 1-minute owner lock before tapped corpses open to everyone.
- **HUD and player UX:** Added a second action bar, separated player debuffs from buffs, buff/debuff effect tooltips, right-click buff cancellation, targetable dead pets for menu recovery, improved aura classification, improved floating combat text plumbing, and a footer link to the public whitepaper.
- **Performance:** Fixed open-world travel freezes with constant point-light counts and render prewarming, added a generic async compile gate for new content, added crowd-adaptive character LOD for populated zones, reused render animation buffers, and throttled arena snapshot readouts.
- **Admin and moderation:** Rebuilt the admin dashboard around Svelte 5 and added moderation detail pages, account/IP association tooling, blocked IP flows, chat filter management, moderation history, provider usage, bug report views, and broad test coverage.
- **Native and release packaging:** Added OTA update support, store polish, iOS/Android metadata updates, app icons, Capacitor live-updates dependency, and mobile release documentation.
- **Architecture:** Shrunk the Sim coordinator behind a SimContext seam, split `IWorld` into facet interfaces, extracted large HUD/render view cores and painters, moved CSS into structured `src/styles` layers, and added standing architecture, parity, UI purity, perf, accessibility, and localization guards.
- **Localization and docs:** Added 7 more locale overlays, refreshed translated README/CONTRIBUTING docs, filled release-gate locale coverage, regenerated resolved i18n outputs, and documented the new i18n, frontend, QA, graphics fairness, and refactor rules.

## Gameplay and Combat

- Projectile damage now resolves when the projectile impacts the target rather than when the cast starts. This fixes the classic-feel timing problem where a spell could land before the visible projectile arrived.
- Added projectile travel modeling and tests for impact timing, no-escape behavior, and chart/screenshot generation.
- Spells now use resist outcomes rather than miss outcomes. This makes spell combat line up with classic-era rules and separates spell resolution from physical hit tables.
- Added Spell Power as an Intellect-derived caster damage stat, with spell-scaling support, tooltip/readout plumbing, tests, and screenshot tooling.
- Added debuff attack power handling into player attack power for PvP so attack power reductions apply correctly against players.
- Added stun diminishing returns to stop chain-stun lockouts in PvP and other repeated-control scenarios.
- Lightning Shield is capped at 3 charges with a 5-second internal cooldown, including charge display/supporting tests and screenshots.
- Direct damage is now the only damage that can move a mob's leash anchor, preventing Lightning Shield from enabling unlimited kiting.
- Hunter aspects are mutually exclusive, with tests covering aspect replacement and exclusion behavior.
- Wolf Form now swings at the rogue cadence instead of using the carried staff cadence.
- Rogue Stealth and Vanish now apply the intended 50% movement speed behavior, and stealth movement penalties were aligned in content.
- Fleeing mob speed is capped at the player base run speed, preventing runaway flee behavior.
- Fleeing mobs no longer socially aggro nearby allies, avoiding cascading social pulls from flee state.
- Hunter and warlock pets despawn when their owner dies.
- Dead pets can be targeted by their owner so the pet menu remains usable for recovery flows.
- Ability cooldowns and combat-potion cooldowns now persist across logout.
- Parked mana for shapeshifted druids persists on save.
- Armor type coverage is now required in the sim/content rules, closing missing-equipment-category gaps.
- Raised the concurrent dungeon/delve instance cap to 24.
- Added and updated focused tests for spell power, spell scaling, spell resist, projectile travel, stun diminishing returns, thorns/Lightning Shield charges, aspect exclusion, form swing cadence, cooldown persistence, dead target selection, pet owner death despawn, mob flee speed, social aggro, PvP safety, and armor type coverage.

## Items, Loot, and Rewards

- Added a full item level system with stat-budget normalization, including item-level computation, interface toggle support, screenshots, and focused tests.
- Added item set bonuses for caster, strength, and agility gear, with tooltip/view support and tests.
- Item tooltips now show armor type, covering cloth, leather, mail, and related item display paths.
- Added master loot distribution support, including sim/server/UI command surfaces and tests for master-loot flows.
- Tapped corpse loot now opens to all players after a 1-minute owner lock, allowing group cleanup without permanently blocking access.
- Tied loot rolls are randomized rather than deterministically favoring one side.
- Loot probability validation was added so bad loot tables are caught by tests instead of shipping silently.
- World Market state now flushes atomically when a player leaves, reducing persistence risk around logout/leave flows.
- Added item-set, item-level, loot-free-for-all, master-loot, loot-roll, armor-type, and save/market test coverage.

## Content and Quest Fixes

- Korzul is no longer chained from Velkhar in a way that forces repeated Sanctum runs.
- Added zone/content updates across `src/sim/content/classes.ts`, dungeon data, temple content, warlock pet data, zone 2, zone 3, talents, classic talents, items, delve items, and item sets.
- Refreshed README and documentation screenshots, including new captures for projectile behavior, flee behavior, item levels, item sets, Lightning Shield, second action bar, dead pet targeting, and travel/weather scenes.
- Added or updated design documentation for spell balance and the master spec.

## HUD, UI, and Player-Facing UX

- Added a second action bar, including keybind/settings support, loadout tests, screenshot coverage, and mobile/style adjustments.
- Player debuffs are now separated from buffs into their own HUD row, making harmful effects easier to scan.
- Aura hover tooltips now show buff/debuff effects rather than just generic labels.
- Players can right-click their own buffs to cancel them.
- Added aura classification and aura effect view helpers to support clearer buff/debuff display.
- Added armor type display on item tooltips.
- Added item set tooltip view support.
- Added ability damage UI helpers and tests.
- Added target buff command coverage.
- Improved party frame painting and related UI parity tests.
- Added screen-reader and accessibility infrastructure across HUD windows, dialogs, focus management, live regions, roving keyboard navigation, forced-colors support, skip links, cast bar progress bars, target/player frame labels, social tabs, bags, market, talents, options, map, arena, quest log, spellbook, leaderboard, and per-window dialog roots.
- Added a shared FocusManager and dialog-root helper, then routed more windows through those primitives.
- Extracted many HUD cold windows and per-frame elements into pure view cores plus thin painters, including options, talents, social, bags, market, character paperdoll, map, arena, quest log, spellbook, leaderboard, cast bars, player/target/party unit frames, action bar, auras, minimap markers, floating combat text, and delve map pieces.
- Added UI purity allowlists and tests to make sure these pure cores stay DOM-free and deterministic where intended.
- Added a footer link to the public whitepaper PDF and included the whitepaper artifact under `public/`.
- Fixed the landing trailer so the poster does not flash before playback.
- Fixed multiple focus, inert, dialog, target-size, forced-colors, live-region, bag prompt, wallet focus-return, arena focus-return, and gamepad remap accessibility issues.

## Ability Icons and Visual Assets

- Converted class ability icons from PNG to WebP, reducing the class ability icon payload to about 924 KB from roughly 5 MB of PNGs.
- Updated per-class skill icon mappings for druid, hunter, mage, paladin, priest, rogue, shaman, warlock, and warrior.
- Added tests for skill icon resolution and aura icon coverage.
- Added weapon rendering on the mech skin.
- Added app icon assets for iOS dark and tinted variants.
- Added refreshed screenshots and performance reference images.

## Rendering and Performance

- Fixed open-world travel freezes by keeping point-light counts constant and prewarming heavy render paths.
- Added a generic async-compile gate so new content does not freeze on first use.
- Added crowd-adaptive character LOD to preserve frame rate in populated zones.
- Reused a single `AnimState` buffer instead of allocating one per entity per frame.
- Throttled arena snapshot readout to reduce server and client pressure.
- Added snapshot self-serialization gating, server tick profiling, and crowd diagnostics.
- Added prewarm, crowd FPS, server load jitter, freeze-walk debug, and client load/perf scripts.
- Added static graphics preset tier knobs for HUD cadence, aura visible counts, floating combat text caps, nameplate refresh cadence, and device-aware defaults.
- Added graphics fairness tests to keep settings gameplay-neutral: lower tiers may reduce cosmetic richness but may not hide actionable information such as own debuffs, party/raid health, target cast bars, target health granularity, enemy positions, or aggro positions.
- Hardened first-run graphics detection, including defaulting unrecognized devices to medium instead of ultra.
- Added browser/perf tests for HUD budgets, target sizes, focus indicators, FCT caps, minimap redraw guards, and static-preset fairness.

## Admin, Moderation, and Operations UI

- Rebuilt the admin dashboard using Svelte 5, with a dedicated `src/admin/` app, routes, state, components, styles, and `svelte-check` type checking.
- Added admin pages for accounts, account detail, blocked IPs, bug reports, characters, chat filter, IP associations, moderation, moderation detail, overview, shared IPs, and provider usage.
- Added reusable admin components for account indicators, account links/modals/notes/status, moderation actions/history/prompts, chat moderation controls, IP block dialogs, online tables, charts, pager, panels, screenshots, stat cards, and word lists.
- Added admin auth/session/polling state modules.
- Added admin i18n catalogs, locale overlays, and generated resolved admin locale outputs.
- Added server/admin database and moderation support for account/IP tooling, chat filters, blocked IPs, shared IPs, provider usage, bug reports, and live shared IP state.
- Added extensive admin test coverage across account indicators, account IPs, account notes/status, app shell, auth flow, blocked IPs, bug reports, characters table, chat filter, confirm dialogs, IP associations, IP blocks, moderation actions/controls/history, navigation, overview, pager, player pages, provider usage, shared IPs, database paths, and live shared IP behavior.

## Bot Detection and Security

- Added private bot detector overlay support in Docker builds so the detector can be present before the application image is built.
- Added runtime bot-detector snapshots and suspicious-player exposure for operators.
- Added bot-detector contract/stub updates and tests.
- Bounded `decodeBase58` input length before decoding to avoid unbounded work from oversized inputs.
- Added or refreshed privacy/security reviewer workflow docs and QA hooks.
- Preserved the rule that secrets stay outside the repo and runtime `.env` remains host-managed.

## Native Apps and Store Readiness

- Added Capacitor live updates via `@capacitor/live-updates`.
- Updated Capacitor configuration for native release behavior.
- Updated Android Capacitor Gradle/settings metadata.
- Updated iOS project metadata, Swift package resolution, app icons, and Capacitor package configuration.
- Added `docs/mobile-store-release.md` for native release/store operational notes.

## Architecture and Refactors

- Introduced and expanded the SimContext seam, then moved major simulation systems behind it while keeping the deterministic sim coordinator thinner.
- Extracted or reorganized sim systems for damage, healing, combat auras, casting lifecycle, effect dispatch, auto attack, mob target selection, mob locomotion, mob death lifecycle, mob on-hit affixes, dungeon instancing, delve runs, delve companion AI, lockpicking, party/raid state, duel/arena, quest credit, quest commands, interaction verbs, inventory/vendor commands, pet AI, pet commands/lifecycle, targeting/raid markers, progression/talents, XP/prestige, chat/player trade, World Market, loot rolls, corpse loot, party loot, Fiesta, Nythraxis, entity roster, and tick prologue.
- Added a committed golden-trace parity harness and a broad set of parity scenarios/goldens for sim behavior across key systems.
- Split `IWorld` into facet interfaces behind the aggregate API, covering combat, targeting, loot, roster, telemetry, progression, talents, cosmetics, pet, party, trade, duel/arena, social graph, market, dungeons, delves, and related world surfaces.
- Added world API structural parity and purity gates, command-schema parity, snapshot round-trip coverage, event-frame parity, and delta-key contract tests.
- Added docs for the world API facet map, command/delta recipes, and server runtime handoff.
- Added architecture/refactor docs describing the v0.15 to v0.16 refactor state.

## Frontend Modernization and CSS

- Moved the CSS stack into `src/styles`, including tokens, base, layout, components, HUD, mobile HUD, shell, and per-entry extra layers.
- Declared a single CSS `@layer` order and added barrel/import guards for build correctness.
- Switched Vite CSS processing to Lightning CSS with browserslist targets.
- Added a build-time backdrop-filter survival check.
- Added structured style docs in `src/styles/CLAUDE.md`.
- Extracted inline HTML style blocks into imported CSS modules.
- Added browser/test coverage for CSS corpus completeness, per-entry rotate-device wiring, shell assertions, backdrop survival, and layer order.
- Added UI effects profile support and debounced `data-fx-level`/CSS token application.
- Removed planning leftovers and codified shipped frontend decisions into docs.

## Localization

- Expanded locale coverage by adding 7 locale overlays: Danish, Dutch, Indonesian, Polish, Swedish, Turkish, and Vietnamese, bringing the touched locale file set to 21.
- Filled release-gate locale coverage for the new UI, admin, ability, HUD, and documentation strings.
- Refreshed translated README and CONTRIBUTING docs across locale-specific files.
- Regenerated resolved i18n outputs, hashes, pending/status summaries, admin i18n outputs, and release-tier artifacts.
- Added and refined i18n build, scan, PR-tier, and release-tier guard behavior.
- Added documentation for the English-only PR carve-out and release-tier full locale requirements.

## Documentation and Agent Workflow

- Collapsed AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md into thin pointers to the canonical CLAUDE.md guidance.
- Updated root and per-directory CLAUDE.md files for post-refactor sim, UI, style, frontend, and QA rules.
- Added `docs/design/graphics-settings-fairness.md` and expanded graphics fairness requirements.
- Added QA gate documentation and hooks, including stop-hook checks for em/en dashes, emoji, `.only`, and debugger usage.
- Added or refreshed `.claude` agents for architecture review, cross-platform sync, migration safety, privacy/security review, QA checklist, and release malware audit.
- Added skills for extract-and-test, feature planning, and maintainer-style PR review.
- Added a changed-files Biome CI ratchet and documented why whole-repo Biome remains deferred.

## Build, Test, and Tooling

- Added `check:ts`, `check:admin`, and `check:types` scripts.
- Added browser test support with `@vitest/browser`, Playwright, and axe-core.
- Added `test:browser` script.
- Added asset conversion script for skill icons.
- Added performance scripts for prewarm and server load checks.
- Added Svelte, Svelte testing, jsdom, sharp, Lightning CSS, Playwright, and Svelte check dependencies.
- Pinned Vitest-related versions in the touched package metadata.
- Updated Docker build inputs to include browserlist/backdrop survival and private bot detector requirements.
- Added `.browserslistrc`, updated `.dockerignore`, and added `private/.dockerkeep`.
- Added or updated hundreds of tests, including sim unit tests, parity golden tests, admin tests, UI/painter tests, CSS tests, browser/accessibility tests, render tests, server/database tests, bot detector tests, and localization tests.

## Deployment and Operations

- Dev has been deployed from `a81443fc` to `b66d1f67` on `world-of-claudecraft-dev` using the Ansible deploy repo, targeting `release/v0.16.0`.
- The dev deploy updated the private bot-protection overlay, built the stack, backed up the database, ran the Old Cragmaw pelt migration dry run and apply path with 0 rows updated, restarted the stack, and passed health checks.
- Public dev health after deploy returned `{"ok":true,"realm":"Claudemoon","players_online":0,"names":[]}`.
- The release tag itself points at the merged main commit for PR #1043, not the pre-merge dev-deployed branch commit.

## Upgrade Notes

- Deploy with the release tag or merged main commit for production/PBE once ready. Dev was deployed from the release branch tip before the final release-check unblock commit and main merge.
- Run deploy-time backups and data migrations using the existing deploy playbook path. The Old Cragmaw pelt migration remains part of the deployment flow and is safe when it has no rows to update.
- Native builds should account for the new Capacitor live-updates dependency and updated iOS/Android metadata.
- Admin deployments now include the Svelte 5 admin bundle and `svelte-check` validation path.
- Docker builds should include the private bot detector overlay where enabled before `docker compose build`.
- The icon conversion materially changes checked-in class skill assets from PNG to WebP, so caches and CDN rules should treat those as new asset paths.
- Operators should expect larger generated i18n/admin artifacts and many regenerated locale files.

## Validation

- Release PR: #1043, `release/v0.16.0` into `main`, merged 2026-06-29.
- Target commit: `7c353ba971ddf8003c3dde4a07ac7ea91bd7c694`.
- PR checks passed before merge: changed-file Biome lint, English-only PR gate, and full 14-locale release gate.
- Dev deployment passed with backup, migration, stack restart, local health check, and public `/api/status` check.
- Previous release baseline: `v0.15.0`.

## Size

Compared with v0.15.0:

- **1,182 files changed**
- **310,395 additions / 54,691 deletions**
- Major changed areas: `src/`, `public/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `server/`, `.claude/`, native iOS/Android metadata, package/build configuration, and deployment packaging.

## Major PRs and Workstreams Included

- #1043 Release v0.16.0
- #1042 Whitepaper footer link
- #1041 WebP class ability icons
- #1018 Admin moderation tools, UX improvements, and Svelte 5 rewrite
- #1015 Generic render async-compile gate

- #999 Wolf Form swing cadence fix
- #998 Item level system
- #997 Stealth and Vanish movement speed
- #996 Dead pet targeting

- #962 Hunter aspect exclusivity
- #959 Base58 decode length bound
- #957 Atomic World Market flush on player leave
- #955 IWorld facet split

## Contributors

patches.name: v0.15.0
patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.15.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.15.0  
**Date:** 2026-06-24  
**Previous release:** v0.14.1

v0.15.0 is a content, delve, rendering, companion API, account polish, admin metrics, and release-stability update with **70 commits** since v0.14.1. The headline is the Delves feature line: deterministic delve runs, lockpicking, reward chests, delve-specific rendering, companions, vendors, maps, docs, and test harnesses. It also ships equipped weapon models in-hand, new weapon icon renders, clickable quest sharing, modifier keybinds, character sorting, one-click vendor junk selling, richer public profile and companion API surfaces, improved admin metrics, wallet/player-card fixes, raid-frame fixes, and full release localization cleanup.

## Highlights

- **Delves and lockpicking** add the new delve run loop, collapsed reliquary content, delve board/map UI, Tumbler's Path lockpick minigame, reward coffers, Marks vendor/shop support, delve companions, affixes, mobs, items, interiors, props, collision fixes, and extensive focused coverage.
- **New content and world polish** adds delve entrance assets, Reliquary Hill labeling, Old Cragmaw loot naming/migration, Nythraxis/Aldric quest coverage, and supporting content docs for the delve system.
- **Equipped weapon rendering** replaces the fixed class-held weapon with the player's equipped mainhand item, backed by roughly 30 weapon model variants and matching rendered 2D bag icons.
- **Gameplay UX improvements** add modifier keybind combos, sortable character select, one-click Sell Junk, clickable quest sharing in chat, Growl autocast, full raid frame ordering, aura stack/debuff display, and a stuck performance-graph fix.
- **Companion API, OAuth, and public profiles** add read-only scoped tokens, OAuth/device flows, character-sheet JSON, crawlable public character pages, generated avatars, public CORS, and character sitemap support.
- **Account, wallet, and player-card hardening** fixes persistent linked-wallet state, uses authoritative live player-card levels, busts stale card-image caches, and closes metadata trust gaps.
- **Admin metrics and site presence** expand admin online/site-user metrics, online-history charts, active/returning account stats, and translated admin dashboards.
- **Release hardening** clears full 14-locale release gates, updates generated i18n artifacts/hashes, and includes the Old Cragmaw migration scripts in the Docker build context.

## Delves, Lockpicking, and New Content

- Added the Delves feature line on top of the v0.15.0 release branch, including deterministic delve simulation, run state, server/client wire support, and content registration.
- Added collapsed reliquary delve content, affixes, delve mobs, delve items, companion behavior, delve shop data, and lockpick tiers.
- Added the delve board/run tracker surfaces plus the delve map and lockpick panel/window UI.
- Added the Tumbler's Path lockpick minigame with server-authoritative command handling, timeout handling, HUD sync, bountiful-jam behavior, and tier-specific generation rules.
- Added reward chest, locked chest, surface exit, pressure plate, cracked grave, destructible wall, portcullis, and other delve interactable rendering paths.
- Added Acolyte Tessa companion scaling by purchased rank rather than always matching the owner level.
- Fixed delve collision so players and mobs are pushed out of solid props while open passages and pressure plates remain walkable.
- Fixed lockpick fairness so generated locks retain a valid legal path through every column.
- Fixed Brother Halven's greeting placeholder so `{playerName}` resolves correctly.
- Added delve content planning/reference docs under `docs/prd/`, including handoff, lockpick minigame, rebuild, and delve design notes.
- Added focused scripts for delve asset failure checks, crypt rendering, module screenshots, wall verification, lockpick E2E, lockpick timer repro, and lockpick UI smoke coverage.

## Weapon Models, Rendering, and Visual Assets

- Equipped weapons now render in-hand based on the player's actual equipped mainhand item rather than a fixed class default.
- The sim-to-wire entity payload carries the render-only mainhand item identity so nearby players see the correct weapon model.
- `CharacterVisual.setWeapon` swaps held models at runtime without rebuilding the whole character or resetting animation state.
- Rogue visuals support replacing both hand slots, while hunter visuals preserve the fixed crossbow behavior.
- Added a shared weapon-variant mapping so 2D bag icons and 3D held models resolve from the same source of truth.
- Added new weapon GLBs for swords, axes, staves, wands, daggers, hammers, spear, scythe, halberd, and advanced class/quality variants.
- Added rendered JPG weapon icons for the new advanced and special weapon variants.
- Added tests covering weapon model/icon existence, model URL resolution, per-class weapon slots, grip-family coverage, and snapshot wire restoration.

## Gameplay, UI, and Social Improvements

- Added modifier keybind combos for edge actions, supporting Shift/Ctrl/Alt/Meta chords while keeping movement modifier-agnostic.
- Added character-select sorting by level, name, recently played, and playtime, with the selected sort persisted locally.
- Added one-click vendor **Sell Junk** for poor-quality non-quest junk items while preserving buyback records.
- Added clickable quest sharing in chat via shift-click or `/share`, with server-side party membership and quest eligibility revalidation.
- Fixed shared quest accept flows so required fallback quest items are re-granted the same way as NPC accepts.
- Added pet Growl autocast behavior and UI/control support.
- Restored full raid frame ordering.
- Fixed Sunder Armor and other target debuffs so they display on the target frame and show aura stack counts.
- Fixed the performance frame-time graph getting stuck at expanded width after switching metric sets.
- Mirrored quest-link, aura-stack, and other UI parity updates into `play.html`.

## Companion API, OAuth, Profiles, and SEO

- Added read-only scoped companion tokens with additive token schema support.
- Hardened the full-token mutation gate so read-scoped tokens cannot be used for mutating routes.
- Added owner and public character-sheet JSON endpoints, with public sheets omitting sensitive stats, vitals, gold, position, inventory, and quest-log data.
- Added OAuth authorization-code + PKCE and device-code grants for companion app access.
- Added companion token create/list/revoke account-portal support.
- Added crawlable public character profile pages at `/c/:name` with OG/Twitter metadata.
- Added deterministic generated character avatars under `/avatar/:class/:skin.png`.
- Added public character sitemap and `robots.txt` sitemap coverage.
- Opened CORS only for public companion/profile/avatar surfaces while preserving the narrow allowlist elsewhere.

## Account, Wallet, Player Cards, and Market Fixes

- Fixed linked wallet persistence across reloads so restored sessions refresh wallet-link status before disconnecting an auto-reconnected wallet.
- Fixed stale player-card shared levels by using authoritative live serialized sim level, then persisted state, then the denormalized character column.
- Versioned shared card image URLs using card update time so social previews can refresh after republish.
- Hardened player-card metadata handling so client-owned level parameters are no longer trusted.
- Redacted console email fallback recipients and kept legacy direct email setters closed in favor of verified email-change flow.
- Keyed market sellers by character id to avoid seller identity ambiguity.

## Admin Metrics and Operations

- Expanded admin metrics database, server, and UI coverage.
- Added site presence plumbing and translated admin labels for online accounts/site users.
- Added online-history chart ranges for 24h, 7d, and 30d.
- Added active account, returning account, peak online, average playtime, and site-user-now metrics.
- Added formatting helpers and chart updates for the expanded admin dashboard.

## Data Migration and Deployment Packaging

- Added the Old Cragmaw pelt migration (`scripts/migrate_old_cragmaw_pelt.ts` and `scripts/old_cragmaw_pelt_migration.ts`).
- The migration renames/moves legacy Old Cragmaw pelt data across character and market storage where needed.
- Updated the server build and Docker context allowlist so the migration scripts are present during Docker builds.
- Dev deployment validation previously ran the migration dry-run/apply path successfully on `world-of-claudecraft-dev` before the final CI/localization commits.

## Internationalization

- Added and regenerated UI/admin translations for the new delve, lockpick, weapon, admin metric, quest sharing, character sort, vendor, companion, and profile strings.
- Filled the release-blocking localization gaps across the full 14-locale release gate.
- Regenerated resolved i18n tables, pending maps, status summaries, and the resolved SHA-256 baseline.
- Current release artifacts report zero pending i18n rows in the release gates.

## Tests, Scripts, and Tooling

- Added or expanded focused tests for delves, delve companions, delve colliders, delve rendering, delve shop, delve map, lockpick generation/step/timeout/window/panel/HUD sync, held weapon models, companion read API, OAuth, character sheets, avatars, account portal companion tokens, admin metrics, character sorting, quest sharing, market seller identity, Old Cragmaw migration, wallet behavior, player-card uploads, performance graph behavior, and raid frame ordering.
- Added screenshot/verification scripts for delve modules, delve walls, lockpick flows, Cragmaw drops, and performance graph reset behavior.
- Updated Biome changed-file configuration and formatted the touched admin locale files so release-to-main CI passes cleanly.

## Upgrade Notes

- This release includes a deploy-time data migration for Old Cragmaw pelt naming/storage. Run the relevant migration step from the deployment playbook before starting the new game container.
- Docker builds require the two Old Cragmaw migration TypeScript files in the build context; this release includes the `.dockerignore` allowlist.
- Operators should expect expanded admin metrics payloads and site-presence fields.
- Companion integrations can use read-only scoped tokens and OAuth/device flows; read tokens are intentionally rejected from mutating routes.
- The release adds many binary model/icon assets for weapon rendering, so checkout and artifact size increase.

## Validation

- Created from `release/v0.15.0` at `c983a4fb`.
- Compared against `v0.14.1`.
- PR #920 checks are green: changed-file Biome lint, English-only PR gate, and full 14-locale release gate.
- Local release-tier validation passed: `I18N_RELEASE_TIER=1 npm test` with **372 test files / 3894 tests passed**.
- Dev deployment of the release line ran successfully earlier on `world-of-claudecraft-dev` with the Old Cragmaw migration dry-run/apply path and healthy `/api/status`; final branch head adds localization and formatting fixes on top.

## Size

Compared with v0.14.1:

- **70 commits**
- **274 files changed**
- **46,341 additions / 8,916 deletions**

patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.14.1 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.14.1  
**Date:** 2026-06-24  
**Previous release:** v0.14.0

v0.14.1 is a gameplay-polish, account-security, UI architecture, raid/party, rendering-performance, and release-stability update with **218 commits** since v0.14.0. It adds two-factor account security and email/data-export flows, themeable and more modular UI systems, better inventory/bag tooling, raid lockout visibility, party/raid roster fixes, Travel Form visual feedback, shader prewarming for crowded scenes, and a broad set of release QA fixes for mobile, `play.html`, Zone 1 content, loot, targeting, and deployment packaging.

## Highlights

- **Account security upgrades** add two-factor authentication, verified email change, recovery-code styling, and account data export from the home page.
- **Transactional and marketing email architecture** establishes the app-side foundation for verification, unsubscribe, and future email workflows.
- **Themeable in-game UI** adds presets and custom palette support, with AA readability fixes across themes.
- **Inventory and HUD modularization** starts the UI extraction work with modular bag filtering and the Vendor window extraction pattern.
- **Party, raid, and lockout improvements** add a minimap raid-lockout badge, raid roster wire fixes, and raid-to-party conversion for small raids.
- **Travel Form and rendering polish** keeps classic +40% speed while adding speed-illusion VFX and shader prewarming to reduce crowd/zone-load stutter.
- **Gameplay reliability fixes** address loot rights, loot-roll redelivery, Tab targeting, stationary mob melee reach, Bound Guardian resummons, and rejoin behavior after kicks.
- **Release hardening** fixes Docker context packaging, `play.html` parity, mobile tutorial copy, native attestation config, release branch CI, and app version metadata.

## Account, Email, and Security

- Added two-factor authentication from the account portal.
- Added verified email-change flow and account data export support.
- Hardened 2FA per security review, including recovery-code and key presentation improvements.
- Added transactional and marketing email architecture.
- Fixed exact-match email verification and unsubscribe GET routes.
- Reclaimed character names abandoned by deactivated accounts.
- Mirrored 2FA login-field behavior into `play.html` and removed dead 2FA helper paths.
- Surfaced every server kick to the client so kicked players can rejoin cleanly.

## UI, HUD, Inventory, and Theming

- Added themeable in-game UI with presets and custom palette support.
- Fixed theme readability so all presets, including Parchment, meet WCAG AA expectations.
- Added modular inventory and bag filtering.
- Established the HUD window-extraction architecture and migrated Vendor first.
- Applied review fixes to the Vendor window extraction.
- Repositioned chat input above the chat box, added autosize behavior, and reduced password-manager interference.
- Added bag-filter CSS parity, mobile input sizing, and empty-bag bar fixes.
- Kept mobile character windows on-screen in both the main client and `play.html`.
- Explained realm population bands with tooltip support.
- Centered the raid convert action and fixed dungeon/raid label overlap on the world map.

## Party, Raid, Loot, and Social Gameplay

- Added `/assist <name>` to target a party member's target.
- Added raid lockout projection through `IWorld` and a minimap lockout badge with unlock countdown.
- Fixed raid roster wire data so raid flags and subgroups appear correctly in the Social panel.
- Added raid-leader conversion from small raid back to party.
- Re-delivered open loot rolls through self snapshots.
- Retired resolved loot rolls and covered the reconcile decision with tests.
- Kept downed party members eligible for loot and XP rights.
- Gave Old Cragmaw a unique trophy and waist drop, and aligned Cragmaw's Huntcord stats/sell value with peers.

## Gameplay, Combat, Targeting, and Zone Content

- Fixed stationary mobs hitting passersby outside melee reach.
- Improved Tab targeting around near-cluster enemy selection and added a dev-only target-cone overlay.
- Allowed the Bound Guardian to be re-summoned after despawning unkilled.
- Removed Zone 1 quest chains and orphaned Brightwood mobs as part of the Zone 1 cleanup.
- Moved Brightwood wildlife south to reduce content overlap.
- Added a Zone 1 quest graph generator for content audit work.
- Kept autorun running while the Escape game menu is open.
- Paginated the lifetime-XP leaderboard for more than 100 level-20 players.
- Widened the RL environment to all nine classes and validated `player_class`.
- Restored demon pet creation helpers after release branch merge repair.

## Druid, Movement, Rendering, and Performance

- Restored Travel Form to classic +40% movement.
- Added speed-illusion VFX for Travel Form without changing the server-side classic speed model.
- Derived Travel Form speed cues from interpolated self position and tightened the cue behavior after review.
- Added focused Travel Form screenshot and diagnostic harnesses.
- Prewarmed entity shaders to reduce crowd and zone-load freezes.
- Frustum-culled off-screen characters while preserving skinned rig visibility behavior.
- Smoothed camera-driven player facing instead of snapping.
- Pointer-locked the canvas during camera rotation so the cursor cannot escape.
- Reset drag/lock state when toggling Mouse Camera mid-drag.
- Released WebGL contexts on real page teardown while avoiding bfcache breakage.

## Mobile, Native, `play.html`, and Release QA

- Registered the iOS attestation plugin and passed the Play Integrity cloud project number for Android.
- Filled mobile tutorial touch-copy translations for all locales.
- Made mobile tutorial copy handedness-neutral and mode-toggle aware.
- Showed touch controls in the new-adventurer tutorial on mobile.
- Synced `play.html` HUD, cast-bar, performance overlay, account portal, chat input, and client-entry parity fixes.
- Guarded homepage account portal behavior on focused client entries.
- Added release-QA locale fixes for v0.14.1 and regenerated i18n status artifacts.
- Synced package, Android, and iOS app versions to 0.14.1.

## Build, CI, SEO, and Deployment Packaging

- Added Biome formatter/linter with a changed-files CI ratchet.
- Added core-pattern overrides and ignored generated i18n summary artifacts where needed.
- Skipped the Biome ratchet for release-to-main PRs.
- Included guide HTML and wiki build scripts in the Docker build context.
- Strengthened official-site SEO signals.
- Added app-version bump automation across package.json, Android, and iOS manifests.

## Upgrade Notes

- App version metadata is now synchronized to 0.14.1 across the web package and native manifests.
- Docker packaging now includes guide HTML and wiki build scripts required by the current build path.
- Native app consumers should account for the iOS attestation plugin registration and Android Play Integrity cloud project number configuration.
- Release-to-main PRs use a special Biome ratchet path so release branches can merge without unrelated changed-file ratchet failures.
- Account/email operators should expect expanded 2FA, verified-email, unsubscribe, and data-export behavior.

## Validation

- Created from `release/v0.14.1` at `c52b607d`.
- Compared against `v0.14.0` at `80d14370`.
- Includes 218 commits across 246 changed files.
- The dev deployment of the v0.14.1 release line completed successfully and returned a healthy `/api/status`; final release-head commits after that deployment are release-to-main CI and official-site SEO metadata changes.

## Size

Compared with v0.14.0:

- **218 commits**
- **246 files changed**
- **93,022 additions / 56,410 deletions**

# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.14.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.14.0  
**Date:** 2026-06-23  
**Previous release:** v0.13.0

v0.14.0 is a guide/wiki, telemetry, anti-bot/security, native mobile hardening, controls, UI, and gameplay-polish release with **82 commits** since v0.13.0. It ships the first curated in-browser guide at `/wiki`, expands gameplay telemetry and server challenge support, hardens native app attestation, improves mobile/desktop interface detection, and fixes several input, combat, aura, and pet behavior edge cases.

## Highlights

- **Curated in-browser guide/wiki** is now available at `/wiki`, with generated content and sitemap coverage for core game knowledge.
- **Gameplay telemetry and APM** now surface richer client performance data through the performance overlay.
- **Challenge protocol support** adds server-triggered client challenges for anti-bot and abuse-prevention workflows.
- **Bot-detector tracking context** now includes command content and session metadata.
- **Native mobile app attestation** is required for supported mobile app environments.
- **Desktop/mobile interface detection** has been tightened, with an Interface Mode override for players.
- **Input and combat reliability fixes** address right-click auto-attack and fast keyboard jump taps.
- **Gameplay polish** includes mutually-exclusive weapon imbues, image-based aura icons, and AFK pet farming prevention.

## Guide / Wiki

- Added the curated in-browser guide/wiki experience at `/wiki`.
- Updated the homepage Wiki navigation to route directly to the guide.
- Added generated guide content for classes, zones, dungeons, pets, families, and creature models.
- Added wiki content generation to the build pipeline.
- Added guide/wiki sitemap generation so guide pages are discoverable.
- Documented the guide/wiki sync contract for future content updates.

## Telemetry, APM, and Challenge Protocol

- Added gameplay telemetry and APM data to the performance overlay.
- Added protocol support for server-triggered client challenges.
- Updated the anti-bot seam to observe command content.
- Passed session metadata into the bot-detector tracking context.

## Native Mobile and Interface Fixes

- Required mobile app attestation for native mobile clients.
- Fixed desktop browsers incorrectly booting into touch UI.
- Added an Interface Mode override for manual UI mode control.
- Fixed the Options language picker so the dropdown opens downward and avoids clipping.

## Input, Combat, and Gameplay Fixes

- Restored right-click auto-attack behavior on hostile mobs.
- Latched keyboard jump handling so fast taps stop randomly failing.
- Made weapon imbues mutually exclusive in a single slot.
- Gated aggressive pet auto-pull on owner activity to prevent AFK pet farming.

## UI, Icons, SEO, and Tooling

- Switched buff/debuff aura icons to image-based skill art.
- Added `/merch` to the sitemap for search and GEO discovery.
- Added release malware audit tooling, including the skill, agent, and scanner.

## Upgrade Notes

- Builds now include guide/wiki content generation as part of the release workflow.
- Native mobile environments need valid attestation configuration.
- Anti-bot and moderation workflows can consume richer command, challenge, telemetry, and session metadata.
- Players who see incorrect mobile/desktop UI detection can use the new Interface Mode override.

## Validation

- Created from `release/v0.14.0` at `80d14370`.
- Compared against `v0.13.0` at `58507cf6`.
- Includes 82 commits across 155 changed files.
- Release branch PR: #863.

- **Insertions:** 30,835
- **Deletions:** 440

patches.name: v0.13.0
patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft - v0.13.0 Release Notes

**Release:** v0.13.0  
**Date:** 2026-06-22  
**Previous release:** v0.12.0

v0.13.0 is a major native mobile, UI/controls, rendering, bug-reporting, and gameplay-polish release with **151 commits** since v0.12.0. It adds iOS/Android app scaffolding, gamepad support, per-character keybinds, ability icon assets, the in-game bug report flow, broad HUD/UX improvements, native/mobile support pages, and several release-critical gameplay fixes including low-quality loot roll behavior and Travel Form diagnostics.

## Highlights

- **Native mobile app support**: adds Capacitor configuration plus generated iOS and Android project shells for native app packaging.
- **Gamepad support**: adds controller input mapping, settings, tests, and screenshot harnesses.
- **Ability icon overhaul**: ships image-based, hand-authored ability icons for all nine classes.
- **In-game bug reporting**: adds a Report a Bug form with screenshot capture, server validation, admin review UI, and screenshot-on-demand viewing.
- **New player and quest UX**: adds a guided new-adventurer tutorial and collapsible quest tracker.
- **Controls and HUD polish**: adds per-character keybind profiles, MMO-themed controls, global UI scale, movable/resizable chat, click feedback, and better accessibility behavior.
- **Rendering/performance improvements**: adds adaptive browser effects, terrain-draped selection rings, world-map prewarming, and entity flicker reduction.
- **Native/mobile and web support pages**: adds store/support docs, support and data-deletion pages, and a `/merch` coming-soon page.
- **Gameplay fixes**: pets path around obstacles, click-to-move pauses on Escape, quest items can be re-granted, Sunken Bastion ward stone pickup is fixed, and poor/common loot no longer opens need/greed rolls.
- **Travel Form investigation support**: adds focused movement coverage and admin live diagnostics for aura/movement multiplier inspection.

## Native Mobile Support

- Added Capacitor configuration for native packaging.
- Added generated Android project files, Gradle wrapper/configuration, app manifest, launcher assets, splash assets, and native activity shell.
- Added generated iOS project files, app delegate, Xcode project/workspace metadata, app icon assets, splash assets, storyboard files, and plist configuration.
- Added support/data deletion pages and mobile store release documentation.
- Updated package scripts/dependencies needed by the native build flow.
- Improved mobile/tablet touch detection so tablet users get the appropriate control path.

## Controls, Input, and Accessibility

- Added game controller support with mapping helpers and tests.
- Added per-character keybinding profiles instead of account-wide-only bindings.
- Added fallback/migration behavior for legacy or malformed keybind storage.
- Added MMO-themed native controls for sliders, dropdowns, spinners, resize handles, and date pickers.
- Added global UI scale support.
- Added a landing/accessibility pass for readability, static mobile backdrop behavior, and better focus/touch affordances.
- Added a dropdown navigation helper and coverage.
- Fixed Escape/menu behavior so click-to-move pauses instead of cancelling a run.
- Added tests and screenshot harnesses around input, keybinds, gamepad, and Escape-run behavior.

## HUD, Quest, Chat, and Player UX

- Added a guided new-adventurer tutorial for first-character onboarding.
- Added a collapsible quest tracker with persisted state and localized count/hints.
- Added movable and resizable desktop chat window behavior.
- Mirrored new chat/bug-report/quest UI CSS into `play.html` where needed.
- Added OSRS-style click feedback marker.
- Added explicit unequip-to-empty-slot support with paperdoll controls, right-click/drag interactions, and accessibility fixes.
- Added live skin-swap hardening so delayed skin assets update materials correctly.
- Added ability icon assets and mappings across class kits.
- Added a `/merch` coming-soon page and updated sitemap/legal/support pages.

## Bug Reporting and Admin

- Added in-game bug report capture with screenshot support.
- Validates screenshot payloads server-side with an allowlist for storable image data.
- Clamps bug report metadata server-side to a bounded shape.
- Returns `screenshotStored` so the client can report when a screenshot was dropped.
- Admin bug report tooling now lists reports with status/meta and fetches screenshots on demand.
- Admin screenshot lightbox supports Escape dismissal and keyboard focus.
- Added admin online diagnostics for active auras, movement multiplier, dry run speed, and swimming state.

## Rendering and Performance

- Added adaptive browser-effects tiers based on browser engine, version, mobile state, and graphics tier.
- Added a Browser Effects graphics setting for manual override.
- Reduced expensive backdrop filters/blur layers on lower tiers.
- Added world-map terrain prewarming during idle time so opening the map avoids a large synchronous paint.
- Added terrain-draped selection rings so target rings remain legible on slopes.
- Added entity interest/visibility hysteresis fixes to reduce boundary flicker.
- Added renderer and screenshot harnesses for browser effects, selection ring, click marker, map prewarm, and visibility behavior.

## Gameplay and Simulation Fixes

- Pets now path around obstacles while heeling instead of getting stuck and eventually warping to the owner.
- Pets that are no longer fighting no longer block owner health regeneration.
- Tab targeting now prioritizes on-screen and engaged enemies instead of off-screen candidates.
- Quest item fallback can re-grant missing quest items from prior quests when accepting related quests.
- Idle Bound Guardian cleanup/despawn behavior was tightened.
- Sunken Bastion overworld ward stone pickup was fixed.
- Brightwood Glade mobs were spread out and Ranger Elwyn's post was cleared.
- Abandon quest confirmation was stabilized.
- Loot roll tooltips now layer above roll panels.
- Poor/common corpse drops now loot directly instead of opening need/greed rolls; uncommon and above still use need/greed.
- Travel Form tests now cover normal movement, follow movement, swimming, Prowl cancellation, and admin-side live diagnostics.

## Server, Account, and Session Behavior

- Added character takeover flow so players can reclaim a character already in the world.
- Added confirmation before taking over a live character session.
- Fixed force-rename gating so rename state is only enforced when `force_rename` is set.
- Kept rename state consistent during the force-rename race.
- Tightened server-side character/session handling around takeover and entry failures.

## Internationalization

- Added and regenerated i18n catalog/resolved/status artifacts for the new UI, admin, bug-report, tutorial, quest tracker, native/control, and release strings.
- Release artifacts are regenerated with pending counts cleared.
