default controls, custom cursors, target markers, personal quest loot, arena
leaderboard health, and fishing.

## Known Notes

- Mouse Camera currently lives under `Options -> Key Bindings`.
- Fishing is a first gameplay slice, not the full professions/crafting feature set.
- Broader public guild directory/request-to-join flow is not included.
- Broader player-facing leaderboards/rankings remain larger than the arena
leaderboard realm-scope fix.

patches.name: v0.4.0
patches.body:
# World of ClaudeCraft v0.4 Release Notes

Branch: `release/v0.4`  
Release head: `4278d5d`  
Baseline: `v0.3.0` / current production `main`

## Highlights

- Adds **The Ashen Coliseum**, a ranked 1v1 arena with matchmaking, Elo ratings, arena UI, leaderboard support, and dedicated test coverage.
- Adds the **World Market** through the Merchant, including player listings, buying, cancelling, collections, expiry handling, persistence, and market test/visual scripts.
- Delivers the major **mobile browser control and layout pass**, including touch controls, safe-area-aware HUD placement, Safari keyboard recovery, mobile login/character flows, mobile map/panel sizing, and landscape handling.
- Reworks the public **homepage/login/start flow** with a localized, responsive shell, realm/character views, project stats, SEO metadata, and PWA manifest.
- Adds an nginx-facing **server unavailable fallback page** so players see a branded maintenance/downstream-error screen instead of a raw proxy failure.
- Includes a large batch of combat, moderation, security, social, performance, and UX fixes from the v0.4 release stack.

## Gameplay

### Ashen Coliseum Arena

- Adds a 1v1 ranked arena queue.
- Adds matchmaking by closest available rating.
- Teleports matched players into a private arena instance.
- Adds a countdown before each arena match.
- Heals and resets combatants for a fair match start.
- Ends arena fights at 1 HP so nobody dies.
- Returns players to their previous world position after the match.
- Adds Elo-style rating movement with persistent wins/losses.
- Adds arena queue, match, countdown, result, and ladder state to the sim/world API.
- Adds an arena panel and `G`/arena button entry point.
- Adds `/api/arena/leaderboard`.
- Adds arena visual and unit coverage.

### World Market

- Adds the Merchant-backed World Market.
- Adds market listings for player-sold goods.
- Adds buying market listings.
- Adds cancelling your own listings.
- Adds expired-listing collection.
- Adds seller collection of copper/items.
- Adds house-stocked market listings so the market is not empty.
- Persists market state through world state storage.
- Adds market UI and market-specific simulation/API coverage.
- Adds `scripts/market_visual.mjs` and `scripts/market_mp_e2e.mjs`.

### Combat And World Fixes

- Fixes Raptor Strike cooldown not applying correctly on queued hunter swings.
- Fixes tamed beasts respawning hostile instead of getting stuck neutral.
- Cleanses hostile NPC control auras correctly.
- Silences noisy mob leash-reset chat.
- Repositions player and party frames to avoid target/UI overlap.
- Adds high-visibility party indicators on minimap and party frames.
- Adds clearer party state indicators, including combat/dead/out-of-range state.
- Improves player/party frame placement for crowded UI states.

### Quest, Vendor, And Starter Flow

- Adds quest accept range enforcement so quests cannot be accepted remotely.
- Clarifies quest and vendor rejection feedback when an interaction fails.
- Gates starter-zone hints on quest state so hints are less noisy.
- Updates starter quest hint test coverage around NPC proximity.
- Keeps the HUD out of the login/start shell.

## Mobile And Browser UX

- Adds dedicated mobile touch controls.
- Adds left movement joystick and right camera joystick.
- Adds bottom mobile action controls: attack, target, chat, and more.
- Adds a mobile overflow menu for secondary panels/actions.
- Moves and scales the XP/cast bars for mobile.
- Moves meters above the XP/cast bars on mobile and constrains overflow.
- Scales down map and major game windows on mobile.
- Adds a close button to the mobile map.
- Makes spellbook and game menu narrower on mobile.
- Centers and raises social UI appropriately on mobile.
- Keeps bottom controls from overlapping the main forward view.
- Adjusts community/Discord/GitHub buttons for mobile.
- Adds mobile-specific character select tabs so character list and creation are usable on narrow screens.
- Centers Play Online / Play Offline cards on mobile.
- Converts GitHub/Discord footer links to compact icon-only buttons on mobile.
- Adds viewport stabilization for iOS Safari so the game does not permanently shrink after the chat keyboard opens.
- Adds Safari keyboard recovery behavior for chat.
- Adds safe-area-aware positioning.
- Adds landscape/fullscreen guidance for mobile gameplay.
- Adds a browser fullscreen setting.
- Adds a PWA manifest with fullscreen display mode.

## Homepage, Login, And Localization

- Rebuilds the homepage/start shell with a richer responsive layout.
- Adds realm, login, and character-selection view routing.
- Adds a client-side view manager.
- Adds dynamic localization infrastructure.
- Adds English and Spanish localization coverage.
- Adds localization tags and dynamic localized attributes in the start shell.
- Adds a language switcher.
- Adds layout auto-centering for start-flow panels.
- Adds project stats API helpers and UI integration.
- Adds homepage SEO metadata, canonical URL, Open Graph metadata, Twitter card metadata, and JSON-LD VideoGame structured data.
- Adds homepage verification, SEO audit, and E2E scripts.
- Adds `public/woc_logo_square.webp` for metadata/PWA usage.

## Social And Multiplayer

- Awaits guild broadcasts so members reliably receive updates.
- Adds social/guild test coverage improvements.
- Indexes character typeahead search for faster social lookup.
- Caches chat censor terms for better chat moderation performance.
- Adds more robust chat/report/admin moderation support.

## Moderation, Auth, And Safety

- Fixes moderation queue status typing so TypeScript accepts the admin moderation row contract.
- Resolves another moderation queue status assertion failure.
- Runs moderation writes on a pinned pooled client so multi-query moderation actions are atomic.
- Requires moderation reasons for suspend/ban style actions.
- Adds account moderation action persistence.
- Adds offensive username/character-name blocking.
- Normalizes character names server-side instead of trusting the client.
- Scopes character deletion to the current realm/account.
- Adds character deletion tests for realm/account isolation.
- Stops buffering oversized request bodies.
- Adds request-size protection coverage.
- Adds safer server-side validation around auth and character flows.

## Performance

- Indexes game sessions by character id.
- Reuses position objects in hot paths.
- Caches chat censor terms.
- Indexes character typeahead search.
- Adds project stats caching/helpers for homepage display.

## Deployment And Ops

- Adds a branded `public/server-unavailable.html` fallback page.
- Adds server-unavailable test coverage.
- Updates Vite proxy targets to `127.0.0.1`.
- Adds PWA manifest and metadata needed for mobile install/fullscreen behavior.
- Keeps build targets passing for environment server, game server, and client.

## Test And Verification Coverage

New or expanded coverage includes:

- Arena simulation and visual script coverage.
- Market simulation, multiplayer E2E, and visual script coverage.
- Homepage foundation, SEO, and verification scripts.
- Mobile control mapping tests.
- Character deletion and realm scoping tests.
- Database search/performance tests.
- Game-session lookup tests.
- HTTP request utility tests.
- Moderation DB tests.
- Security/request-size tests.
- Server unavailable fallback tests.
- Social system tests.
- Quest/vendor/starter hint regression tests.
- Combat and world regression tests for Raptor Strike, hostile NPC auras, leash chat, and tamed beast respawn behavior.

Verified before release promotion:

- `DATABASE_URL=postgres://test/test npm test` passed: 35 test files, 397 tests.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
- `npm run build:env` passed.
- `npm run build:server` passed.
- `npm run build` passed.
- `npm ci` completed with 0 vulnerabilities in the merge-check worktree.

## Not Included Yet

These PRs were still open against `release/v0.4` after the current release head and are not part of this release branch snapshot:

- #121 refresh character window after equipping from bags.
- #123 drag spellbook abilities onto the hotbar.
- #124 full action-bar spell replacement regression coverage.
- #125 vendor stack-selling QoL.
- #127 clearing/removing action-bar slots.
- #129 food and drink action-bar shortcuts.

Those should be treated as follow-up release candidates unless they are explicitly merged before promotion.

patches.name: v0.3.0
patches.body:
## World of Claudecraft v0.3.0

This release is a major online-play update focused on social systems, moderation,
combat behavior, performance, and the first-pass release polish for the public game.

### Highlights

- Added friends, guilds, ignores, and realm-aware social systems.
- Added player reporting and admin moderation tools.
- Added right-click reporting from chat player names.
- Added a classic MMO-style threat system for tanking and deaggro.
- Polished the title screen, login flow, character creation, and character preview.
- Added hashed media caching for faster repeat loads.
- Added in-game community links.
- Included recent trade, chat, options, movement, and performance fixes.

### Social

- Added a Friends panel with add/remove, presence, zone/status display, and whisper
shortcuts.
- Added ignore/block support, with blocked players filtered server-side.
- Added guild creation, invites, ranks, guild chat, officer chat, promote/demote,
kick, leader transfer, leave, and disband flows.
- Added WoW-style realm list support, with realm-scoped characters, guilds, friends,
and character names.

### Moderation

- Players can now report other players in game.
- Players can right-click chat names to report, ignore, whisper, friend, invite to
party, or invite to guild.
- Chat right-click menus intentionally exclude Trade and Challenge to Duel, since
those are live nearby-player actions.
- Reports are resolved server-side by live player id or realm-scoped character name.
- Admin moderation tools now support report review, ignore report, suspend, ban, and
force rename.
- Reports include recent chat context where available.

### Combat And Gameplay

- Added a classic threat model so mobs can change targets based on damage, healing,
taunts, pets, and tank actions.
- Added deaggro/leash behavior so mobs do not stay locked forever.
- Added tank-oriented behavior for warriors, druids, paladins, and hunter pets.
- Preserved rogue stealth behavior, including stealth visibility/detection handling.
- Improved immediate bag refresh after trade.
- Fixed trade item duplication issues.
- Added invite lockout protections so party, duel, and trade invites do not
overwrite each other unexpectedly.

### Chat

- Added separate chat and combat log windows.
- Improved scrollback behavior.
- Added chat rate limiting and ignore actions.
- Persisted accepted chat logs to Postgres with retention.
- Logged accepted chat channels only.

### UI And Options

- Polished the title screen and online/offline entry flow.
- Added 3D character preview.
- Improved class selection hover/focus behavior.
- Added a game options menu for key bindings, graphics, and audio.
- Improved character select layout.
- Added in-game community invite links.

### Performance And Infrastructure

- Added hashed media caching for safer long-lived asset caching.
- Added retained hashed media output under `/media`.
- Improved movement jitter behavior.
- Added identity deltas, retained entity lists, and distance-tiered update rates to
reduce town/crowd lag.
- Added spatial hash grid support for entity radius queries.

### Admin Notes

- New admin moderation views are available through the admin dashboard.
- Production deploys should include generated hashed media assets.
- The release was integrated through `release/v0.3` and promoted through `dev-0.3`
to `main`.

### Known Notes

- Electron desktop builds are not part of this release.
- Mobile support is not a focus for this release.
